simon_sinek_how_great_leaders_inspire_ac.en: Why would we use that verb, it doesn't "feel" right? Because the part of the brain that controls decision-making doesn't control language. The best we can muster up is,
simon_sinek_how_great_leaders_inspire_ac.zh: 而我們最多能整理出的是〝我不知道，就是覺得不對〞

simon_sinek_how_great_leaders_inspire_ac.en: "I don't know. It just doesn't feel right." Or sometimes you say you're leading with your heart or soul. I hate to break it to you, those aren't other body parts controlling your behavior.
simon_sinek_how_great_leaders_inspire_ac.zh: 嗯，我很不想對你說，那些都不是身體的其他部份

simon_sinek_how_great_leaders_inspire_ac.en: It's all happening here in your limbic brain, the part of the brain that controls decision-making and not language. But if you don't know why you do what you do,
simon_sinek_how_great_leaders_inspire_ac.zh: 所有的發生都在你邊緣的腦裡

simon_sinek_how_great_leaders_inspire_ac.en: and people respond to why you do what you do, then how will you ever get people to vote for you, or buy something from you, or, more importantly, be loyal
simon_sinek_how_great_leaders_inspire_ac.zh: 而人們對為什麼你正在做的事有反應 然後甚至是你如何贏得人們 的選票或買你的東西

simon_sinek_how_great_leaders_inspire_ac.en: and want to be a part of what it is that you do. The goal is not just to sell to people who need what you have; the goal is to sell to people who believe what you believe.
simon_sinek_how_great_leaders_inspire_ac.zh: 而且想成為你正在做的事的一份子 再一次，目標不是僅僅將需要你所有賣給需要的人而已

simon_sinek_how_great_leaders_inspire_ac.en: The goal is not just to hire people who need a job; it's to hire people who believe what you believe. I always say that, you know,
simon_sinek_how_great_leaders_inspire_ac.zh: 目標不是只在雇 而是要雇用那些相信你所相信的事的人 我會一直這麼說，你知道。

simon_sinek_how_great_leaders_inspire_ac.en: if you hire people just because they can do a job, they'll work for your money, but if they believe what you believe, they'll work for you with blood and sweat and tears.
simon_sinek_how_great_leaders_inspire_ac.zh: 但如果你雇用那些相信你所相信的人們

simon_sinek_how_great_leaders_inspire_ac.en: Nowhere else is there a better example than with the Wright brothers. Most people don't know about Samuel Pierpont Langley. And back in the early 20th century,
simon_sinek_how_great_leaders_inspire_ac.zh: 多數人不認識Samuel Pierpont Langley.

simon_sinek_how_great_leaders_inspire_ac.en: the pursuit of powered man flight was like the dot com of the day. Everybody was trying it. And Samuel Pierpont Langley had, what we assume, to be the recipe for success. Even now, you ask people,
simon_sinek_how_great_leaders_inspire_ac.zh: 追尋人類飛翔就像今天的網路公司一樣  而Samuel Pierpont Langley曾經擁有，我們假設的 成功的關鍵

simon_sinek_how_great_leaders_inspire_ac.en: "Why did your product or why did your company fail?" and people always give you the same permutation of the same three things: under-capitalized, the wrong people, bad market conditions.
simon_sinek_how_great_leaders_inspire_ac.zh: 「為什麼你的產品或你的公司失敗了？」人們總是給你同樣的三件事的相同排列：資本不足、錯誤的人、糟糕的市場狀況。

simon_sinek_how_great_leaders_inspire_ac.en: It's always the same three things, so let's explore that. Samuel Pierpont Langley was given 50,000 dollars by the War Department to figure out this flying machine. Money was no problem.
simon_sinek_how_great_leaders_inspire_ac.zh: 從國防部得到5萬美金 去研發這個飛行機器 資金不是問題

simon_sinek_how_great_leaders_inspire_ac.en: He held a seat at Harvard and worked at the Smithsonian and was extremely well-connected; he knew all the big minds of the day. He hired the best minds money could find
simon_sinek_how_great_leaders_inspire_ac.zh: 在Smithsonian工作而且有很好的人脈  他雇用最好的人才

simon_sinek_how_great_leaders_inspire_ac.en: and the market conditions were fantastic. The New York Times followed him around everywhere, and everyone was rooting for Langley. Then how come we've never heard of Samuel Pierpont Langley?
simon_sinek_how_great_leaders_inspire_ac.zh: 而市場情況呈現一片榮景 紐約時報跟著他到處跑 而且每個人都為Langley加油 那麼你怎麼會從沒聽過Samuel Pierpont Langley？

simon_sinek_how_great_leaders_inspire_ac.en: A few hundred miles away in Dayton, Ohio, Orville and Wilbur Wright, they had none of what we consider to be the recipe for success. They had no money;
simon_sinek_how_great_leaders_inspire_ac.zh: 他們沒有我們認為的 他們沒有資金

simon_sinek_how_great_leaders_inspire_ac.en: they paid for their dream with the proceeds from their bicycle shop. Not a single person on the Wright brothers' team had a college education, not even Orville or Wilbur.
simon_sinek_how_great_leaders_inspire_ac.zh: 他們用他們腳踏車店裡的現金來支付他們的夢想   兄弟倆都沒有

simon_sinek_how_great_leaders_inspire_ac.en: And The New York Times followed them around nowhere. The difference was, Orville and Wilbur were driven by a cause, by a purpose, by a belief.
simon_sinek_how_great_leaders_inspire_ac.zh: 而他們也沒有紐約時報的報導 不同的是 萊特兄弟是有個目的去驅動他們的

simon_sinek_how_great_leaders_inspire_ac.en: They believed that if they could figure out this flying machine, it'll change the course of the world. Samuel Pierpont Langley was different. He wanted to be rich, and he wanted to be famous.
simon_sinek_how_great_leaders_inspire_ac.zh: 他們相信如果他們 這將改變世界的軌道

simon_sinek_how_great_leaders_inspire_ac.en: He was in pursuit of the result. He was in pursuit of the riches. And lo and behold, look what happened. The people who believed in the Wright brothers' dream
simon_sinek_how_great_leaders_inspire_ac.zh: 他在追求結果 他在追求財富 所以你們看，看看發生了什麼

simon_sinek_how_great_leaders_inspire_ac.en: worked with them with blood and sweat and tears. The others just worked for the paycheck. They tell stories of how every time the Wright brothers went out, they would have to take five sets of parts,
simon_sinek_how_great_leaders_inspire_ac.zh: 另外那些人則只是為錢工作 而他們述說的故事是每次萊特兄弟出門

simon_sinek_how_great_leaders_inspire_ac.en: because that's how many times they would crash before supper. And, eventually, on December 17th, 1903, the Wright brothers took flight, and no one was there to even experience it.
simon_sinek_how_great_leaders_inspire_ac.zh: 而在場甚至沒有其他人可以體驗它

simon_sinek_how_great_leaders_inspire_ac.en: We found out about it a few days later. And further proof that Langley was motivated by the wrong thing: the day the Wright brothers took flight, he quit. He could have said,
simon_sinek_how_great_leaders_inspire_ac.zh: 幾天後我們才知道這件事。進一步證明蘭利的動機是錯的：萊特兄弟起飛的那一天，他退出了。他本來可以說，

simon_sinek_how_great_leaders_inspire_ac.en: "That's an amazing discovery, guys, and I will improve upon your technology," but he didn't. He wasn't first, he didn't get rich, he didn't get famous, so he quit.
simon_sinek_how_great_leaders_inspire_ac.zh: 〝那真是很驚人的發現啊 而我將在你們的技術上加以改良〞，但他沒有。 他跑第一，但他沒有變有錢

simon_sinek_how_great_leaders_inspire_ac.en: People don't buy what you do; they buy why you do it. If you talk about what you believe, you will attract those who believe what you believe.
simon_sinek_how_great_leaders_inspire_ac.zh: 你會吸引那些相信你所相信的人們

simon_sinek_how_great_leaders_inspire_ac.en: But why is it important to attract those who believe what you believe? Something called the law of diffusion of innovation, if you don't know the law, you know the terminology.
simon_sinek_how_great_leaders_inspire_ac.zh: 但為什麼吸吲那些相信你所相信的人們很重要？

simon_sinek_how_great_leaders_inspire_ac.en: The first 2.5% of our population are our innovators. The next 13.5% of our population are our early adopters. The next 34% are your early majority, your late majority and your laggards.
simon_sinek_how_great_leaders_inspire_ac.zh: 接下來的13.5%

simon_sinek_how_great_leaders_inspire_ac.en: The only reason these people buy touch-tone phones is because you can't buy rotary phones anymore. (Laughter) We all sit at various places at various times on this scale,
simon_sinek_how_great_leaders_inspire_ac.zh: 因為你不能再買到轉盤式的電話了  在這個尺度上我們都坐在不同的位置，不同的時間上

simon_sinek_how_great_leaders_inspire_ac.en: but what the law of diffusion of innovation tells us is that if you want mass-market success or mass-market acceptance of an idea, you cannot have it until you achieve this tipping point
simon_sinek_how_great_leaders_inspire_ac.zh: 但創意的散播法則告訴我們的是 如果你想要在大眾市場成功 你不能擁有它

simon_sinek_how_great_leaders_inspire_ac.en: between 15 and 18 percent market penetration, and then the system tips. I love asking businesses, "What's your conversion on new business?" They love to tell you, "It's about 10 percent," proudly.
simon_sinek_how_great_leaders_inspire_ac.zh: 在15％到18％之間的市場占有率的引爆點 然後系統就會一面倒到你這邊 我喜歡問企業：在新事業上你們的轉換率是多少

simon_sinek_how_great_leaders_inspire_ac.en: Well, you can trip over 10% of the customers. We all have about 10% who just "get it." That's how we describe them, right? That's like that gut feeling, "Oh, they just get it."
simon_sinek_how_great_leaders_inspire_ac.zh: 嗯，你可能會絆倒超過 10% 的顧客。我們都有大約 10% 的人只是「明白了」。我們就是這樣描述它們的，對嗎？這就像直覺，“哦，他們明白了。”

simon_sinek_how_great_leaders_inspire_ac.en: The problem is: How do you find the ones that get it before doing business versus the ones who don't get it? So it's this here, this little gap that you have to close,
simon_sinek_how_great_leaders_inspire_ac.zh: 問題是：如何找到在開展業務之前就獲得了它的人和沒有獲得它的人？所以這就是這裡，你必須彌補的這個小差距，

simon_sinek_how_great_leaders_inspire_ac.en: as Jeffrey Moore calls it, "Crossing the Chasm" -- because, you see, the early majority will not try something until someone else has tried it first.
simon_sinek_how_great_leaders_inspire_ac.zh: 如同Jeffrey Moore稱它為〞跨越分歧  直到其他人

simon_sinek_how_great_leaders_inspire_ac.en: And these guys, the innovators and the early adopters, they're comfortable making those gut decisions. They're more comfortable making those intuitive decisions
simon_sinek_how_great_leaders_inspire_ac.zh: 而這些人，這些發明家以及早期的採用者 他們對做這些需要膽試的決定感到很自在

simon_sinek_how_great_leaders_inspire_ac.en: that are driven by what they believe about the world and not just what product is available. These are the people who stood in line for six hours to buy an iPhone when they first came out,
simon_sinek_how_great_leaders_inspire_ac.zh: 也就是他們被他們自己對世界的相信所驅動 而不僅只是可得的產品 這些是排隊排了六個小時

simon_sinek_how_great_leaders_inspire_ac.en: when you could have bought one off the shelf the next week. These are the people who spent 40,000 dollars on flat-screen TVs when they first came out, even though the technology was substandard.
simon_sinek_how_great_leaders_inspire_ac.zh: 這些是花40，000美金  雖然科技還未臻完美

simon_sinek_how_great_leaders_inspire_ac.en: And, by the way, they didn't do it because the technology was so great; they did it for themselves. It's because they wanted to be first. People don't buy what you do; they buy why you do it
simon_sinek_how_great_leaders_inspire_ac.zh: 而且，順便提一下，他們這麼做並不 他們這麼做是為了他們自己 因為他們想當第一 人們不買你做什麼；他們買你為什麼做。

simon_sinek_how_great_leaders_inspire_ac.en: and what you do simply proves what you believe. In fact, people will do the things that prove what they believe. The reason that person bought the iPhone in the first six hours,
simon_sinek_how_great_leaders_inspire_ac.zh: 事實上，人們會去做 為什麼人們買iPhone

simon_sinek_how_great_leaders_inspire_ac.en: stood in line for six hours, was because of what they believed about the world, and how they wanted everybody to see them: they were first. People don't buy what you do; they buy why you do it.
simon_sinek_how_great_leaders_inspire_ac.zh: 排隊排六個小時 是因為他們所相信的世界

simon_sinek_how_great_leaders_inspire_ac.en: So let me give you a famous example, a famous failure and a famous success of the law of diffusion of innovation. First, the famous failure. It's a commercial example.
simon_sinek_how_great_leaders_inspire_ac.zh: 所以讓我說一個有名的例子 一個有名的失敗以及有名的成功  它是個商業的例子

simon_sinek_how_great_leaders_inspire_ac.en: As we said before, the recipe for success is money and the right people and the right market conditions. You should have success then. Look at TiVo.
simon_sinek_how_great_leaders_inspire_ac.zh: 如同我們之前所說，一秒鐘前

simon_sinek_how_great_leaders_inspire_ac.en: From the time TiVo came out about eight or nine years ago to this current day, they are the single highest-quality product on the market, hands down, there is no dispute.
simon_sinek_how_great_leaders_inspire_ac.zh: 當TiVo上市時，約8，9年前  他們仍是市場上有高品質的產品

simon_sinek_how_great_leaders_inspire_ac.en: They were extremely well-funded. Market conditions were fantastic. I mean, we use TiVo as verb. I TiVo stuff on my piece-of-junk Time Warner DVR all the time. (Laughter)
simon_sinek_how_great_leaders_inspire_ac.zh: 我總是TiVo東西在我時代華納的DVR

simon_sinek_how_great_leaders_inspire_ac.en: But TiVo's a commercial failure. They've never made money. And when they went IPO, their stock was at about 30 or 40 dollars and then plummeted, and it's never traded above 10.
simon_sinek_how_great_leaders_inspire_ac.zh: 他們從不賺錢 而當他們股票上市的時候 股價約30到40美金 然後一瀉千里，從沒在10美元以上交易

simon_sinek_how_great_leaders_inspire_ac.en: In fact, I don't think it's even traded above six, except for a couple of little spikes. Because you see, when TiVo launched their product, they told us all what they had.
simon_sinek_how_great_leaders_inspire_ac.zh: 除了幾次小漲幅 因為你看，當TiVo推出他們的產品

simon_sinek_how_great_leaders_inspire_ac.en: They said, "We have a product that pauses live TV, skips commercials, rewinds live TV and memorizes your viewing habits without you even asking." And the cynical majority said, "We don't believe you.
simon_sinek_how_great_leaders_inspire_ac.zh: 他們說〞我們的產品可以暫停現場的電視節目  你連問都不必問  我們不相信

simon_sinek_how_great_leaders_inspire_ac.en: We don't need it. We don't like it. You're scaring us." What if they had said, "If you're the kind of person who likes to have total control over every aspect of your life,
simon_sinek_how_great_leaders_inspire_ac.zh: 我們不需要它，我們不喜歡它。   要是你是這樣的人： 生活的各個層面

simon_sinek_how_great_leaders_inspire_ac.en: boy, do we have a product for you. It pauses live TV, skips commercials, memorizes your viewing habits, etc., etc." People don't buy what you do; they buy why you do it,
simon_sinek_how_great_leaders_inspire_ac.zh: 那麼，我們有這麼一樣適合你的產品 它可以暫停現場節目，跳過商業廣告 記得你的收視習慣等等

simon_sinek_how_great_leaders_inspire_ac.en: and what you do simply serves as the proof of what you believe. Now let me give you a successful example of the law of diffusion of innovation. In the summer of 1963,
simon_sinek_how_great_leaders_inspire_ac.zh: 在1963年夏天

simon_sinek_how_great_leaders_inspire_ac.en: 250,000 people showed up on the mall in Washington to hear Dr. King speak. They sent out no invitations, and there was no website to check the date. How do you do that?
simon_sinek_how_great_leaders_inspire_ac.zh: 25,000人在 準備聽金恩博士演說  而當時也沒有網站去查看日期 你會怎麼做

simon_sinek_how_great_leaders_inspire_ac.en: Well, Dr. King wasn't the only man in America who was a great orator. He wasn't the only man in America who suffered in a pre-civil rights America. In fact, some of his ideas were bad.
simon_sinek_how_great_leaders_inspire_ac.zh: 金恩博士不是在美國唯一一個

simon_sinek_how_great_leaders_inspire_ac.en: But he had a gift. He didn't go around telling people what needed to change in America. He went around and told people what he believed. "I believe, I believe, I believe," he told people.
simon_sinek_how_great_leaders_inspire_ac.zh: 但他有個天賦

simon_sinek_how_great_leaders_inspire_ac.en: And people who believed what he believed took his cause, and they made it their own, and they told people. And some of those people created structures to get the word out to even more people.
simon_sinek_how_great_leaders_inspire_ac.zh: 而那些相信他所相信的人們  而這些人之中的某些人創造了制度

simon_sinek_how_great_leaders_inspire_ac.en: And lo and behold, 250,000 people showed up on the right day at the right time to hear him speak. How many of them showed up for him? Zero. They showed up for themselves.
simon_sinek_how_great_leaders_inspire_ac.zh: 在這個時間、這個地點 有多少人為了他而出現  他們為了他們自己出現

simon_sinek_how_great_leaders_inspire_ac.en: It's what they believed about America that got them to travel in a bus for eight hours to stand in the sun in Washington in the middle of August.
simon_sinek_how_great_leaders_inspire_ac.zh: 那是他們所相信的美國  在八月中時站在華盛頓的陽光下

simon_sinek_how_great_leaders_inspire_ac.en: It's what they believed, and it wasn't about black versus white: 25% of the audience was white. Dr. King believed that there are two types of laws in this world:
simon_sinek_how_great_leaders_inspire_ac.zh: 那是他們所相信的，這不是關於黑人與白人

simon_sinek_how_great_leaders_inspire_ac.en: those that are made by a higher authority and those that are made by men. And not until all the laws that are made by men are consistent with the laws made by the higher authority
simon_sinek_how_great_leaders_inspire_ac.zh: 而一直到所有法律都由一般人所定 與那些較高權威制定的法律一致時

simon_sinek_how_great_leaders_inspire_ac.en: will we live in a just world. It just so happened that the Civil Rights Movement was the perfect thing to help him bring his cause to life. We followed, not for him, but for ourselves.
simon_sinek_how_great_leaders_inspire_ac.zh: 我們追隨，不是為了他，而是為了我們自己

simon_sinek_how_great_leaders_inspire_ac.en: By the way, he gave the "I have a dream" speech, not the "I have a plan" speech. (Laughter) Listen to politicians now, with their comprehensive 12-point plans. They're not inspiring anybody.
simon_sinek_how_great_leaders_inspire_ac.zh: 而且，他的演說是〞我有一個夢想〞   聽現在的政客們完整的12點計劃

simon_sinek_how_great_leaders_inspire_ac.en: Because there are leaders and there are those who lead. Leaders hold a position of power or authority, but those who lead inspire us. Whether they're individuals or organizations,
simon_sinek_how_great_leaders_inspire_ac.zh: 因為有"領導人"與"領導的人" 領導人持有權力 但那些領導的人啟發我們

simon_sinek_how_great_leaders_inspire_ac.en: we follow those who lead, not because we have to, but because we want to. We follow those who lead, not for them, but for ourselves. And it's those who start with "why"
simon_sinek_how_great_leaders_inspire_ac.zh: 而是因為我們想要

simon_sinek_how_great_leaders_inspire_ac.en: that have the ability to inspire those around them or find others who inspire them. Thank you very much. (Applause)
simon_sinek_how_great_leaders_inspire_ac.zh: 擁有這個能力

amy_cuddy_your_body_language_may_shape_w.en: So I want to start by offering you a free no-tech life hack, and all it requires of you is this: that you change your posture for two minutes. But before I give it away, I want to ask you to right now
amy_cuddy_your_body_language_may_shape_w.zh: 首先我要提供你們一個免費的 你只要 改變你的姿勢二分鐘 在我說明前，我要先請大家

amy_cuddy_your_body_language_may_shape_w.en: do a little audit of your body and what you're doing with your body. So how many of you are sort of making yourselves smaller? Maybe you're hunching, crossing your legs, maybe wrapping your ankles.
amy_cuddy_your_body_language_may_shape_w.zh: 先檢視一下你的姿態 你們之中有多少人縮著身體？

amy_cuddy_your_body_language_may_shape_w.en: Sometimes we hold onto our arms like this. Sometimes we spread out. (Laughter) I see you. So I want you to pay attention to what you're doing right now.
amy_cuddy_your_body_language_may_shape_w.zh: 有時我們張開雙臂（笑聲） 我看到囉（笑聲） 現在請大家專心在自己身上

amy_cuddy_your_body_language_may_shape_w.en: We're going to come back to that in a few minutes, and I'm hoping that if you learn to tweak this a little bit, it could significantly change the way your life unfolds.
amy_cuddy_your_body_language_may_shape_w.zh: 我們將在幾分鐘後回到這個問題，我希望如果你學會稍微調整一下，它可以顯著改變你的生活展開方式。

amy_cuddy_your_body_language_may_shape_w.en: So, we're really fascinated with body language, and we're particularly interested in other people's body language. You know, we're interested in, like, you know — (Laughter) —
amy_cuddy_your_body_language_may_shape_w.zh: 所以，我們深為身體語言著迷 特別是 你知道，當我們為 (笑聲)

amy_cuddy_your_body_language_may_shape_w.en: an awkward interaction, or a smile, or a contemptuous glance, or maybe a very awkward wink, or maybe even something like a handshake. Narrator: Here they are arriving at Number 10.
amy_cuddy_your_body_language_may_shape_w.zh: 尷尬的互動，或微笑 或輕蔑的一瞥，或不自然的眨眼 甚至是握手這樣的一件事 （影片旁白）：他們抵達 英國首相官邸唐寧街10號

amy_cuddy_your_body_language_may_shape_w.en: This lucky policeman gets to shake hands with the President of the United States. Here comes the Prime Minister -- No. (Laughter) (Applause) (Laughter) (Applause)
amy_cuddy_your_body_language_may_shape_w.zh: 來自....的首相? 不 (笑聲) (掌聲) (笑聲) (掌聲)

amy_cuddy_your_body_language_may_shape_w.en: Amy Cuddy: So a handshake, or the lack of a handshake, can have us talking for weeks and weeks and weeks. Even the BBC and The New York Times. So obviously when we think about nonverbal behavior,
amy_cuddy_your_body_language_may_shape_w.zh: 我們都可以聊上好幾個禮拜 即使 BBC 和紐約時報也不例外 我們說到非語言行為或身體語言時

amy_cuddy_your_body_language_may_shape_w.en: or body language -- but we call it nonverbals as social scientists -- it's language, so we think about communication. When we think about communication, we think about interactions.
amy_cuddy_your_body_language_may_shape_w.zh: 我們社會科學學者 將之歸類為非口語語言 它就是一種語言 所以我們會想到溝通 當我們想到溝通，我們就想到互動

amy_cuddy_your_body_language_may_shape_w.en: So what is your body language communicating to me? What's mine communicating to you? And there's a lot of reason to believe that this is a valid way to look at this.
amy_cuddy_your_body_language_may_shape_w.zh: 有很多理由讓我們相信 這是有效的切入點

amy_cuddy_your_body_language_may_shape_w.en: So social scientists have spent a lot of time looking at the effects of our body language, or other people's body language, on judgments.
amy_cuddy_your_body_language_may_shape_w.zh: 因此，社會科學家花了很多時間研究我們的肢體語言或其他人的肢體語言對判斷的影響。

amy_cuddy_your_body_language_may_shape_w.en: And we make sweeping judgments and inferences from body language. And those judgments can predict really meaningful life outcomes like who we hire or promote, who we ask out on a date.
amy_cuddy_your_body_language_may_shape_w.zh: 而我們根據他人的身體語言 推論並做出快速又決絕的判斷 這些判斷可以幫我們 預測生命裡很有意義的事件 像是要雇用誰，邀請誰出去約會

amy_cuddy_your_body_language_may_shape_w.en: For example, Nalini Ambady, a researcher at Tufts University, shows that when people watch 30-second soundless clips of real physician-patient interactions, their judgments of the physician's niceness
amy_cuddy_your_body_language_may_shape_w.zh: 舉例而言，Tufts 大學的研究員 Nalini Ambady 讓我們看到   他們對該醫師是否和善的觀感

amy_cuddy_your_body_language_may_shape_w.en: predict whether or not that physician will be sued. So it doesn't have to do so much with whether or not that physician was incompetent, but do we like that person and how they interacted?
amy_cuddy_your_body_language_may_shape_w.zh: 可用來預測該醫師日後 是否會被病人告上法庭 跟這個醫師稱職與否沒有太大關係

amy_cuddy_your_body_language_may_shape_w.en: Even more dramatic, Alex Todorov at Princeton has shown us that judgments of political candidates' faces in just one second predict 70 percent of U.S. Senate and gubernatorial race outcomes,
amy_cuddy_your_body_language_may_shape_w.zh: 更戲劇化的是，普林斯頓 Alex Todorov 的研究告訴我們 我們在一秒內 對政治人物臉部的喜好判斷

amy_cuddy_your_body_language_may_shape_w.en: and even, let's go digital, emoticons used well in online negotiations can lead you to claim more value from that negotiation. If you use them poorly, bad idea. Right?
amy_cuddy_your_body_language_may_shape_w.zh: 甚至在數位的領域裡  可以讓你在協商中獲得較多價值 假如你運用不得當，不妙！對吧?

amy_cuddy_your_body_language_may_shape_w.en: So when we think of nonverbals, we think of how we judge others, how they judge us and what the outcomes are. We tend to forget, though, the other audience
amy_cuddy_your_body_language_may_shape_w.zh: 想到非口語語言時 我們就想到判斷別人  我們往往忘記，還有其他人 也受到我們非口語語言影響

amy_cuddy_your_body_language_may_shape_w.en: that's influenced by our nonverbals, and that's ourselves. We are also influenced by our nonverbals, our thoughts and our feelings and our physiology. So what nonverbals am I talking about?
amy_cuddy_your_body_language_may_shape_w.zh: 我們也被自己的非口語語言所影響： 我們的想法、感覺和生理狀況 所以我說的究竟是甚麼樣的非口語語言?

amy_cuddy_your_body_language_may_shape_w.en: I'm a social psychologist. I study prejudice, and I teach at a competitive business school, so it was inevitable that I would become interested in power dynamics.
amy_cuddy_your_body_language_may_shape_w.zh: 我是一位社會心理學家，我研究偏見 我在一所競爭激烈的商學院教書 因此無可避免地對權力間互動關係著迷

amy_cuddy_your_body_language_may_shape_w.en: I became especially interested in nonverbal expressions of power and dominance. And what are nonverbal expressions of power and dominance? Well, this is what they are.
amy_cuddy_your_body_language_may_shape_w.zh: 特別是在非口語語言表達方面的 權力和支配 展示權力和支配的非口語語言又為何呢?

amy_cuddy_your_body_language_may_shape_w.en: So in the animal kingdom, they are about expanding. So you make yourself big, you stretch out, you take up space, you're basically opening up. It's about opening up.
amy_cuddy_your_body_language_may_shape_w.zh: 在動物世界裡，權力和支配的 非口語語言講究擴展 所以你盡可能得把自己變大 你伸展身體 占滿空間，基本上就是開展身體 就是關於展開身體

amy_cuddy_your_body_language_may_shape_w.en: And this is true across the animal kingdom. It's not just limited to primates. And humans do the same thing. (Laughter) So they do this both when they have power sort of chronically,
amy_cuddy_your_body_language_may_shape_w.zh: 兩者皆是：不論是長期習於掌權

amy_cuddy_your_body_language_may_shape_w.en: and also when they're feeling powerful in the moment. And this one is especially interesting because it really shows us how universal and old these expressions of power are.
amy_cuddy_your_body_language_may_shape_w.zh: 或只在當下覺得權力強大 特別有趣的原因是它真實地呈現

amy_cuddy_your_body_language_may_shape_w.en: This expression, which is known as pride, Jessica Tracy has studied. She shows that people who are born with sight and people who are congenitally blind do this when they win at a physical competition.
amy_cuddy_your_body_language_may_shape_w.zh: Jessica Tracy 研究顯示  和先天視障的人 在贏得比賽時都做同樣的事

amy_cuddy_your_body_language_may_shape_w.en: So when they cross the finish line and they've won, it doesn't matter if they've never seen anyone do it. They do this. So the arms up in the V, the chin is slightly lifted.
amy_cuddy_your_body_language_may_shape_w.zh: 當他們跨過終點線贏得比賽之際

amy_cuddy_your_body_language_may_shape_w.en: What do we do when we feel powerless? We do exactly the opposite. We close up. We wrap ourselves up. We make ourselves small. We don't want to bump into the person next to us.
amy_cuddy_your_body_language_may_shape_w.zh: 讓自己變得小一點 最好別碰到身旁的人

amy_cuddy_your_body_language_may_shape_w.en: So again, both animals and humans do the same thing. And this is what happens when you put together high and low power. So what we tend to do when it comes to power
amy_cuddy_your_body_language_may_shape_w.zh: 同樣，動物和人類都做同樣的事情。這就是當你將高功率和低功率放在一起時會發生的情況。那麼當涉及到權力時我們傾向於做什麼

amy_cuddy_your_body_language_may_shape_w.en: is that we complement the other's nonverbals. So if someone is being really powerful with us, we tend to make ourselves smaller. We don't mirror them. We do the opposite of them.
amy_cuddy_your_body_language_may_shape_w.zh: 若有人對我們展現權力 我們傾向把自己縮小些 我們不模仿他們

amy_cuddy_your_body_language_may_shape_w.en: So I'm watching this behavior in the classroom, and what do I notice? I notice that MBA students really exhibit the full range of power nonverbals.
amy_cuddy_your_body_language_may_shape_w.zh: 當我在課堂上觀察這麼現象時

amy_cuddy_your_body_language_may_shape_w.en: So you have people who are like caricatures of alphas, really coming into the room, they get right into the middle of the room before class even starts, like they really want to occupy space.
amy_cuddy_your_body_language_may_shape_w.zh: 你會看到有些人就像動物群裡 支配的雄性的變形

amy_cuddy_your_body_language_may_shape_w.en: When they sit down, they're sort of spread out. They raise their hands like this. You have other people who are virtually collapsing when they come in. As soon they come in, you see it.
amy_cuddy_your_body_language_may_shape_w.zh: 當他們坐下的時候，身體會展開

amy_cuddy_your_body_language_may_shape_w.en: You see it on their faces and their bodies, and they sit in their chair and they make themselves tiny, and they go like this when they raise their hand. I notice a couple of things about this.
amy_cuddy_your_body_language_may_shape_w.zh: 我觀察到兩點：

amy_cuddy_your_body_language_may_shape_w.en: One, you're not going to be surprised. It seems to be related to gender. So women are much more likely to do this kind of thing than men. Women feel chronically less powerful than men,
amy_cuddy_your_body_language_may_shape_w.zh: 長期以來女人都不覺得像男人那麼強勢

amy_cuddy_your_body_language_may_shape_w.en: so this is not surprising. But the other thing I noticed is that it also seemed to be related to the extent to which the students were participating, and how well they were participating.
amy_cuddy_your_body_language_may_shape_w.zh: 這種表現似乎跟學生的參與程度

amy_cuddy_your_body_language_may_shape_w.en: And this is really important in the MBA classroom, because participation counts for half the grade. So business schools have been struggling with this gender grade gap.
amy_cuddy_your_body_language_may_shape_w.zh: 在 MBA 的課堂上來說這真的非常重要 因為參與的表現占成績的一半

amy_cuddy_your_body_language_may_shape_w.en: You get these equally qualified women and men coming in and then you get these differences in grades, and it seems to be partly attributable to participation. So I started to wonder, you know, okay,
amy_cuddy_your_body_language_may_shape_w.zh: 可是成績出來卻反映性別差異  所以我開始思考，好吧

amy_cuddy_your_body_language_may_shape_w.en: so you have these people coming in like this, and they're participating. Is it possible that we could get people to fake it and would it lead them to participate more?
amy_cuddy_your_body_language_may_shape_w.zh: 這群人一開始來是這副樣子 看起來積極參與  進而影響他們，能更積極參與?

amy_cuddy_your_body_language_may_shape_w.en: So my main collaborator Dana Carney, who's at Berkeley, and I really wanted to know, can you fake it till you make it? Like, can you do this just for a little while
amy_cuddy_your_body_language_may_shape_w.zh: 所以我的主要合作者達納·卡尼（Dana Carney）在伯克利大學，我真的很想知道，你能假裝它直到你成功嗎？就像，你能這樣做一會兒嗎

amy_cuddy_your_body_language_may_shape_w.en: and actually experience a behavioral outcome that makes you seem more powerful? So we know that our nonverbals govern how other people think and feel about us. There's a lot of evidence.
amy_cuddy_your_body_language_may_shape_w.zh: 並實際體驗到讓你看起來更強大的行為結果？所以我們知道，我們的非語言行為決定著其他人對我們的看法和感受。有很多證據。

amy_cuddy_your_body_language_may_shape_w.en: But our question really was, do our nonverbals govern how we think and feel about ourselves? There's some evidence that they do. So, for example, we smile when we feel happy,
amy_cuddy_your_body_language_may_shape_w.zh: 確實有證據支持這個說法 舉例來說，我們開心的時候會微笑

amy_cuddy_your_body_language_may_shape_w.en: but also, when we're forced to smile by holding a pen in our teeth like this, it makes us feel happy. So it goes both ways. When it comes to power, it also goes both ways. So when you feel powerful,
amy_cuddy_your_body_language_may_shape_w.zh: 但同樣地，當我們口中咬住一支筆 強迫呈現微笑的表情時  這互相影響

amy_cuddy_your_body_language_may_shape_w.en: you're more likely to do this, but it's also possible that when you pretend to be powerful, you are more likely to actually feel powerful. So the second question really was, you know,
amy_cuddy_your_body_language_may_shape_w.zh: 你更有可能這樣做，但也有可能當你假裝強大時，你更有可能真正感到強大。所以第二個問題確實是，你知道，

amy_cuddy_your_body_language_may_shape_w.en: so we know that our minds change our bodies, but is it also true that our bodies change our minds? And when I say minds, in the case of the powerful, what am I talking about?
amy_cuddy_your_body_language_may_shape_w.zh: 我們知道心理狀態會影響身體 那身體是否能影響心理呢? 這裡所說的心理充滿力量 指的是甚麼?

amy_cuddy_your_body_language_may_shape_w.en: So I'm talking about thoughts and feelings and the sort of physiological things that make up our thoughts and feelings, and in my case, that's hormones. I look at hormones.
amy_cuddy_your_body_language_may_shape_w.zh: 我指的是想法和感覺 和構成我們想法和感受的生理因素

amy_cuddy_your_body_language_may_shape_w.en: So what do the minds of the powerful versus the powerless look like? So powerful people tend to be, not surprisingly, more assertive and more confident, more optimistic.
amy_cuddy_your_body_language_may_shape_w.zh: 充滿力量或充滿無力感 不出乎意料，感覺自己有力的人往往 比較果斷，自信，且樂觀

amy_cuddy_your_body_language_may_shape_w.en: They actually feel they're going to win even at games of chance. They also tend to be able to think more abstractly. So there are a lot of differences. They take more risks.
amy_cuddy_your_body_language_may_shape_w.zh: 他們確切認為機會永遠站在他們這邊  還有許多差異。這種人比較敢承受風險

amy_cuddy_your_body_language_may_shape_w.en: There are a lot of differences between powerful and powerless people. Physiologically, there also are differences on two key hormones: testosterone, which is the dominance hormone,
amy_cuddy_your_body_language_may_shape_w.zh: 一是睪固酮:也就是支配性荷爾蒙

amy_cuddy_your_body_language_may_shape_w.en: and cortisol, which is the stress hormone. So what we find is that high-power alpha males in primate hierarchies have high testosterone and low cortisol, and powerful and effective leaders
amy_cuddy_your_body_language_may_shape_w.zh: 一是腎上腺皮質醇:也就是壓力荷爾蒙  有大量的睪固酮和低量的腎上腺皮質醇 強勢，高效能的領袖人物

amy_cuddy_your_body_language_may_shape_w.en: also have high testosterone and low cortisol. So what does that mean? When you think about power, people tended to think only about testosterone, because that was about dominance.
amy_cuddy_your_body_language_may_shape_w.zh: 也有大量睪固酮與低量的腎上腺皮質醇 這表示甚麼? 當你想到權力  因為它代表支配統治

amy_cuddy_your_body_language_may_shape_w.en: But really, power is also about how you react to stress. So do you want the high-power leader that's dominant, high on testosterone, but really stress reactive? Probably not, right?
amy_cuddy_your_body_language_may_shape_w.zh: 但力量其實也和如何處理壓力有關 所以你會想見一個有支配地位強勢領袖 有著很多睪固酮但同時又對壓力反應過度嗎? 大概不會吧，不是嗎?你會希望那個人

amy_cuddy_your_body_language_may_shape_w.en: You want the person who's powerful and assertive and dominant, but not very stress reactive, the person who's laid back. So we know that in primate hierarchies, if an alpha needs to take over,
amy_cuddy_your_body_language_may_shape_w.zh: 但也不會對壓力反應過度，不能輕鬆以對 靈長動物的社群階級裡，如果一個強勢雄性

amy_cuddy_your_body_language_may_shape_w.en: if an individual needs to take over an alpha role sort of suddenly, within a few days, that individual's testosterone has gone up significantly and his cortisol has dropped significantly.
amy_cuddy_your_body_language_may_shape_w.zh: 幾天內，他體內的睪固酮一定急速增加 而其腎上腺皮質醇劇烈地減少

amy_cuddy_your_body_language_may_shape_w.en: So we have this evidence, both that the body can shape the mind, at least at the facial level, and also that role changes can shape the mind. So what happens, okay, you take a role change,
amy_cuddy_your_body_language_may_shape_w.zh: 身體影響心理，由此可證 至少就表面而言是如此 同時角色的轉換也會影響心理 所以，如果你改變角色

amy_cuddy_your_body_language_may_shape_w.en: what happens if you do that at a really minimal level, like this tiny manipulation, this tiny intervention? "For two minutes," you say, "I want you to stand like this,
amy_cuddy_your_body_language_may_shape_w.zh: 如果你做一個微小改變會怎樣 像這樣的操作，這樣一個小小的干預?

amy_cuddy_your_body_language_may_shape_w.en: and it's going to make you feel more powerful." So this is what we did. We decided to bring people into the lab and run a little experiment, and these people adopted, for two minutes,
amy_cuddy_your_body_language_may_shape_w.zh: 接著我們就決定做這個實驗。

amy_cuddy_your_body_language_may_shape_w.en: either high-power poses or low-power poses, and I'm just going to show you five of the poses, although they took on only two. So here's one. A couple more.
amy_cuddy_your_body_language_may_shape_w.zh: 無論是高功率姿勢還是低功率姿勢，我只想向您展示其中五個姿勢，儘管它們只呈現了兩個。所以這是一個。還有幾個。

amy_cuddy_your_body_language_may_shape_w.en: This one has been dubbed the "Wonder Woman" by the media. Here are a couple more. So you can be standing or you can be sitting. And here are the low-power poses.
amy_cuddy_your_body_language_may_shape_w.zh: 還有這兩個 或站或坐 這些是無助的姿勢

amy_cuddy_your_body_language_may_shape_w.en: So you're folding up, you're making yourself small. This one is very low-power. When you're touching your neck, you're really protecting yourself. So this is what happens.
amy_cuddy_your_body_language_may_shape_w.zh: 你雙手交叉，試著讓自己變小一點  當你摸脖子 實際的狀況是，他們一進來

amy_cuddy_your_body_language_may_shape_w.en: They come in, they spit into a vial, for two minutes, we say, "You need to do this or this." They don't look at pictures of the poses. We don't want to prime them with a concept of power.
amy_cuddy_your_body_language_may_shape_w.zh: 我們告訴他，擺這個姿勢，兩分鐘 他們不會看到姿勢的照片，因為我們不想要暗示，影響他們

amy_cuddy_your_body_language_may_shape_w.en: We want them to be feeling power. So two minutes they do this. We then ask them, "How powerful do you feel?" on a series of items, and then we give them an opportunity to gamble,
amy_cuddy_your_body_language_may_shape_w.zh: 我們希望他們感受到力量。他們這樣做了兩分鐘。然後我們問他們：「你覺得自己有多強大？」在一系列專案上，然後我們給他們一個賭博的機會，

amy_cuddy_your_body_language_may_shape_w.en: and then we take another saliva sample. That's it. That's the whole experiment. So this is what we find. Risk tolerance, which is the gambling,
amy_cuddy_your_body_language_may_shape_w.zh: 接著再取一次唾液樣本 就是這樣。這就是整個實驗

amy_cuddy_your_body_language_may_shape_w.en: we find that when you are in the high-power pose condition, 86 percent of you will gamble. When you're in the low-power pose condition, only 60 percent, and that's a whopping significant difference.
amy_cuddy_your_body_language_may_shape_w.zh: 擺低權勢姿態的人呢

amy_cuddy_your_body_language_may_shape_w.en: Here's what we find on testosterone. From their baseline when they come in, high-power people experience about a 20-percent increase, and low-power people experience about a 10-percent decrease.
amy_cuddy_your_body_language_may_shape_w.zh: 我們發現 這些人進來的那一刻起，擺高權勢姿態的人  擺低權勢姿態的人則是下降 10%

amy_cuddy_your_body_language_may_shape_w.en: So again, two minutes, and you get these changes. Here's what you get on cortisol. High-power people experience about a 25-percent decrease,
amy_cuddy_your_body_language_may_shape_w.zh: 所以，再說一次，只有兩分鐘，就有這種差異 擺有權勢姿態的人

amy_cuddy_your_body_language_may_shape_w.en: and the low-power people experience about a 15-percent increase. So two minutes lead to these hormonal changes that configure your brain to basically be either assertive, confident and comfortable,
amy_cuddy_your_body_language_may_shape_w.zh: 擺低權勢姿態的人腎上腺皮質醇則上升 15% 只要二分鐘可以讓這些荷爾蒙產生這種改變 使你的腦袋成為

amy_cuddy_your_body_language_may_shape_w.en: or really stress-reactive, and feeling sort of shut down. And we've all had the feeling, right? So it seems that our nonverbals do govern how we think and feel about ourselves,
amy_cuddy_your_body_language_may_shape_w.zh: 或高度緊張以及感到  看來非口語的語言確實掌控

amy_cuddy_your_body_language_may_shape_w.en: so it's not just others, but it's also ourselves. Also, our bodies change our minds. But the next question, of course, is, can power posing for a few minutes really change your life in meaningful ways?
amy_cuddy_your_body_language_may_shape_w.zh: 不只是影響別人，更影響我們自己 同時，我們的身體可以改變心理 但下一個問題，當然，就是 維持數分鐘的姿勢

amy_cuddy_your_body_language_may_shape_w.en: This is in the lab, it's this little task, it's just a couple of minutes. Where can you actually apply this? Which we cared about, of course.
amy_cuddy_your_body_language_may_shape_w.zh: 這是在實驗室裡，這是一個小任務，只需幾分鐘。你可以在哪裡實際應用這個？當然，我們關心這一點。

amy_cuddy_your_body_language_may_shape_w.en: And so we think where you want to use this is evaluative situations, like social threat situations. Where are you being evaluated, either by your friends? For teenagers, it's at the lunchroom table.
amy_cuddy_your_body_language_may_shape_w.zh: 我們認為什麼時候可以應用呢？ 像是社交威脅的情境裡，在你被人評估的時候

amy_cuddy_your_body_language_may_shape_w.en: For some people it's speaking at a school board meeting. It might be giving a pitch or giving a talk like this or doing a job interview. We decided that the one that most people could relate to
amy_cuddy_your_body_language_may_shape_w.zh: 或是找工作面試時，都用的上 我們認為一般人最能認同體會的情境

amy_cuddy_your_body_language_may_shape_w.en: because most people had been through, was the job interview. So we published these findings, and the media are all over it, and they say, Okay, so this is what you do
amy_cuddy_your_body_language_may_shape_w.zh: 因為大多數人都經歷過，就是工作面試。所以我們發表了這些發現，媒體對此進行了報道，他們說，好吧，這就是你所做的

amy_cuddy_your_body_language_may_shape_w.en: when you go in for the job interview, right? (Laughter) You know, so we were of course horrified, and said, Oh my God, no, that's not what we meant at all. For numerous reasons, no, don't do that.
amy_cuddy_your_body_language_may_shape_w.zh: 當你參加工作面試時，對嗎？ （笑聲）你知道，所以我們當然感到震驚，並說，天啊，不，我們根本不是這個意思。由於多種原因，不，不要這樣做。

amy_cuddy_your_body_language_may_shape_w.en: Again, this is not about you talking to other people. It's you talking to yourself. What do you do before you go into a job interview? You do this. You're sitting down. You're looking at your iPhone --
amy_cuddy_your_body_language_may_shape_w.zh: 這和你跟別人交談無關 這是你和你自己心理對談  對吧? 你會做下來，你盯著自己的 iPhone 手機

amy_cuddy_your_body_language_may_shape_w.en: or your Android, not trying to leave anyone out. You're looking at your notes, you're hunching up, making yourself small, when really what you should be doing maybe is this,
amy_cuddy_your_body_language_may_shape_w.zh: 你把自己蜷縮起來，試著讓自己變得小一點

amy_cuddy_your_body_language_may_shape_w.en: like, in the bathroom, right? Do that. Find two minutes. So that's what we want to test. Okay? So we bring people into a lab, and they do either high- or low-power poses again,
amy_cuddy_your_body_language_may_shape_w.zh: 所以我們想測試的是這個  他們再擺高權勢或低權勢的姿態

amy_cuddy_your_body_language_may_shape_w.en: they go through a very stressful job interview. It's five minutes long. They are being recorded. They're being judged also, and the judges are trained to give no nonverbal feedback,
amy_cuddy_your_body_language_may_shape_w.zh: 接著進行一個高度壓力的求職面試 為時五分鐘。全程錄影 他們同時也被評論，而這些考官都接受過訓練

amy_cuddy_your_body_language_may_shape_w.en: so they look like this. Imagine this is the person interviewing you. So for five minutes, nothing, and this is worse than being heckled. People hate this.
amy_cuddy_your_body_language_may_shape_w.zh: 所以他們看起來就像這樣，想像一下  整整五分鐘，甚麼都沒有，這比刁難詰問更難受 大家都不喜歡這種方式。這就是 Marianne LaFrance 所謂的

amy_cuddy_your_body_language_may_shape_w.en: It's what Marianne LaFrance calls "standing in social quicksand." So this really spikes your cortisol. So this is the job interview we put them through, because we really wanted to see what happened.
amy_cuddy_your_body_language_may_shape_w.zh: 這可以大大激發你的腎上腺皮質醇 我們給予受試者這種面試

amy_cuddy_your_body_language_may_shape_w.en: We then have these coders look at these tapes, four of them. They're blind to the hypothesis. They're blind to the conditions. They have no idea who's been posing in what pose,
amy_cuddy_your_body_language_may_shape_w.zh: 我們找了四個打分數的人看這些面試錄影 他們不知假設前提和也不了解測試的條件 沒有人知道誰擺甚麼樣的姿勢

amy_cuddy_your_body_language_may_shape_w.en: and they end up looking at these sets of tapes, and they say, "We want to hire these people," all the high-power posers. "We don't want to hire these people.
amy_cuddy_your_body_language_may_shape_w.zh: 最後他們觀看這些帶子 然後他們說，"噢，我們想要錄用這些人"

amy_cuddy_your_body_language_may_shape_w.en: We also evaluate these people much more positively overall." But what's driving it? It's not about the content of the speech. It's about the presence that they're bringing to the speech.
amy_cuddy_your_body_language_may_shape_w.zh: 我們對這些人的整體印象也比較正面   而是他們把自己帶進了演講裡

amy_cuddy_your_body_language_may_shape_w.en: Because we rate them on all these variables related to competence, like, how well-structured is the speech? How good is it? What are their qualifications?
amy_cuddy_your_body_language_may_shape_w.zh: 我們也衡量一些其他的變數

amy_cuddy_your_body_language_may_shape_w.en: No effect on those things. This is what's affected. These kinds of things. People are bringing their true selves, basically. They're bringing themselves. They bring their ideas, but as themselves,
amy_cuddy_your_body_language_may_shape_w.zh: 這些事。基本上人們表現出真實的自己

amy_cuddy_your_body_language_may_shape_w.en: with no, you know, residue over them. So this is what's driving the effect, or mediating the effect. So when I tell people about this,
amy_cuddy_your_body_language_may_shape_w.zh: 這就是背後真實的力量，或造成影響 所以當我告訴人們

amy_cuddy_your_body_language_may_shape_w.en: that our bodies change our minds and our minds can change our behavior, and our behavior can change our outcomes, they say to me, "It feels fake." Right? So I said, fake it till you make it.
amy_cuddy_your_body_language_may_shape_w.zh: 我們的身體會改變心理，心理會改變行為   我就說，你就假裝一直到你達成目的為止。不幹，這不是我

amy_cuddy_your_body_language_may_shape_w.en: It's not me. I don't want to get there and then still feel like a fraud. I don't want to feel like an impostor. I don't want to get there only to feel like I'm not supposed to be here.
amy_cuddy_your_body_language_may_shape_w.zh: 我不想要成功後感覺像是一個騙子 我不想自己覺得自己是個假貨

amy_cuddy_your_body_language_may_shape_w.en: And that really resonated with me, because I want to tell you a little story about being an impostor and feeling like I'm not supposed to be here. When I was 19, I was in a really bad car accident.
amy_cuddy_your_body_language_may_shape_w.zh: 我深有同感 這裡跟大家分享一個小故事  在我 19 歲的時候，發生了一場很嚴重的車禍

amy_cuddy_your_body_language_may_shape_w.en: I was thrown out of a car, rolled several times. I was thrown from the car. And I woke up in a head injury rehab ward, and I had been withdrawn from college,
amy_cuddy_your_body_language_may_shape_w.zh: 我整個人飛出車外，滾了好幾翻 我彈出車外，頭部重傷，醒來時身處頭部復健病房

amy_cuddy_your_body_language_may_shape_w.en: and I learned that my IQ had dropped by two standard deviations, which was very traumatic. I knew my IQ because I had identified with being smart, and I had been called gifted as a child.
amy_cuddy_your_body_language_may_shape_w.zh: 別人告知我智商下降了 2 個標準差 就是說非常非常糟糕 我知道我的智商應該是多少，因為我以前被人家認為是很聰明的那種 小時大家都教我是資優生

amy_cuddy_your_body_language_may_shape_w.en: So I'm taken out of college, I keep trying to go back. They say, "You're not going to finish college. Just, you know, there are other things for you to do, but that's not going to work out for you."
amy_cuddy_your_body_language_may_shape_w.zh: 當我被迫離開大學時，我試著回去 他們說都告訴我說，"你沒有辦法畢業的。

amy_cuddy_your_body_language_may_shape_w.en: So I really struggled with this, and I have to say, having your identity taken from you, your core identity, and for me it was being smart, having that taken from you,
amy_cuddy_your_body_language_may_shape_w.zh: 當你自我認同感被剝奪，主要的身分認同 對我而言，我的自我認同就是聰明 不再聰明，再沒有比這個讓我更加無助的了

amy_cuddy_your_body_language_may_shape_w.en: there's nothing that leaves you feeling more powerless than that. So I felt entirely powerless. I worked and worked, and I got lucky, and worked, and got lucky, and worked.
amy_cuddy_your_body_language_may_shape_w.zh: 沒有什麼比這更讓你感到無力的了。所以我感到完全無力。我工作又工作，我很幸運，我工作，我很幸運，我工作。

amy_cuddy_your_body_language_may_shape_w.en: Eventually I graduated from college. It took me four years longer than my peers, and I convinced someone, my angel advisor, Susan Fiske, to take me on, and so I ended up at Princeton,
amy_cuddy_your_body_language_may_shape_w.zh: 我比同儕多花了四年的時間  收我，讓我進入了普林斯頓

amy_cuddy_your_body_language_may_shape_w.en: and I was like, I am not supposed to be here. I am an impostor. And the night before my first-year talk, and the first-year talk at Princeton is a 20-minute talk to 20 people. That's it.
amy_cuddy_your_body_language_may_shape_w.zh: 我當時覺得，我不應該在這裡 我是個冒牌貨 在我第一年演講前的那個晚上，

amy_cuddy_your_body_language_may_shape_w.en: I was so afraid of being found out the next day that I called her and said, "I'm quitting." She was like, "You are not quitting, because I took a gamble on you, and you're staying.
amy_cuddy_your_body_language_may_shape_w.zh: 所以我打給她說，"我不幹了。" 她說:"你不可以不幹， 因為我賭在你身上了，你得留下。

amy_cuddy_your_body_language_may_shape_w.en: You're going to stay, and this is what you're going to do. You are going to fake it. You're going to do every talk that you ever get asked to do. You're just going to do it and do it and do it,
amy_cuddy_your_body_language_may_shape_w.zh: 你會留下，你得這麼做。   你得一直講一直講

amy_cuddy_your_body_language_may_shape_w.en: even if you're terrified and just paralyzed and having an out-of-body experience, until you have this moment where you say, 'Oh my gosh, I'm doing it.
amy_cuddy_your_body_language_may_shape_w.zh: 魂不附體了，直到你發現你在說

amy_cuddy_your_body_language_may_shape_w.en: Like, I have become this. I am actually doing this.'" So that's what I did. Five years in grad school, a few years, you know, I'm at Northwestern, I moved to Harvard, I'm at Harvard,
amy_cuddy_your_body_language_may_shape_w.zh: 我已經成為它的一部分了，我就是正牌普林斯頓人。" 這就是說所做的，五年的研究生生涯

amy_cuddy_your_body_language_may_shape_w.en: I'm not really thinking about it anymore, but for a long time I had been thinking, "Not supposed to be here." So at the end of my first year at Harvard,
amy_cuddy_your_body_language_may_shape_w.zh: 但之前有很長一段時間我都在想這件事

amy_cuddy_your_body_language_may_shape_w.en: a student who had not talked in class the entire semester, who I had said, "Look, you've gotta participate or else you're going to fail," came into my office. I really didn't know her at all.
amy_cuddy_your_body_language_may_shape_w.zh: 我曾經警告她："你得參與融入否則你會被當"

amy_cuddy_your_body_language_may_shape_w.en: She came in totally defeated, and she said, "I'm not supposed to be here." And that was the moment for me. Because two things happened. One was that I realized,
amy_cuddy_your_body_language_may_shape_w.zh: "我不配在這裡。"

amy_cuddy_your_body_language_may_shape_w.en: oh my gosh, I don't feel like that anymore. I don't feel that anymore, but she does, and I get that feeling. And the second was, she is supposed to be here! Like, she can fake it, she can become it.
amy_cuddy_your_body_language_may_shape_w.zh: 我再也不這樣感覺了，但她卻是，我懂她。  她可以假裝，她可以成功。

amy_cuddy_your_body_language_may_shape_w.en: So I was like, "Yes, you are! You are supposed to be here! And tomorrow you're going to fake it, you're going to make yourself powerful, and, you know -- (Applause)
amy_cuddy_your_body_language_may_shape_w.zh: 明天起你就假裝  你將會---"(掌聲)

amy_cuddy_your_body_language_may_shape_w.en: And you're going to go into the classroom, and you are going to give the best comment ever." You know? And she gave the best comment ever, and people turned around and were like,
amy_cuddy_your_body_language_may_shape_w.zh: 你會發表最棒的評論。" 你知道嗎? 她就真的發表了最棒的評論 大家都回過神來，他們就好像

amy_cuddy_your_body_language_may_shape_w.en: oh my God, I didn't even notice her sitting there. (Laughter) She comes back to me months later, and I realized that she had not just faked it till she made it,
amy_cuddy_your_body_language_may_shape_w.zh: 天啊，我甚至沒有註意到她坐在那裡。 （笑聲）幾個月後她回到我身邊，我意識到她不僅僅是假裝，直到她做到了，

amy_cuddy_your_body_language_may_shape_w.en: she had actually faked it till she became it. So she had changed. And so I want to say to you, don't fake it till you make it. Fake it till you become it.
amy_cuddy_your_body_language_may_shape_w.zh: 整個人脫胎換骨 我想對大家說，不要假裝到你成功 先假裝然後終將成真。知道嗎?

amy_cuddy_your_body_language_may_shape_w.en: Do it enough until you actually become it and internalize. The last thing I'm going to leave you with is this. Tiny tweaks can lead to big changes. So, this is two minutes.
amy_cuddy_your_body_language_may_shape_w.zh: 持續地做直到它內化到你的骨子裡。 最後與大家分享的是 小調整，大改變 就二分鐘

amy_cuddy_your_body_language_may_shape_w.en: Two minutes, two minutes, two minutes. Before you go into the next stressful evaluative situation, for two minutes, try doing this, in the elevator,
amy_cuddy_your_body_language_may_shape_w.zh: 二分鐘，二分鐘，二分鐘  拿出二分鐘，試試看做這個，電梯裡，

amy_cuddy_your_body_language_may_shape_w.en: in a bathroom stall, at your desk behind closed doors. That's what you want to do. Configure your brain to cope the best in that situation. Get your testosterone up. Get your cortisol down.
amy_cuddy_your_body_language_may_shape_w.zh: 洗手間，關上房門，就在你的桌子前 你就這麼做，規劃你的腦袋  提升你的睪固銅，降低你的腎上腺皮質醇

amy_cuddy_your_body_language_may_shape_w.en: Don't leave that situation feeling like, oh, I didn't show them who I am. Leave that situation feeling like, I really feel like I got to say who I am and show who I am.
amy_cuddy_your_body_language_may_shape_w.zh: 千萬別事後才有，噢，我沒把最好那面表現出來那種遺憾 而是留下，噢，我得以充分表現表達自己

amy_cuddy_your_body_language_may_shape_w.en: So I want to ask you first, you know, both to try power posing, and also I want to ask you to share the science, because this is simple. I don't have ego involved in this. (Laughter)
amy_cuddy_your_body_language_may_shape_w.zh: 同時也想請求各位 這可不是我自大 (笑聲)

amy_cuddy_your_body_language_may_shape_w.en: Give it away. Share it with people, because the people who can use it the most are the ones with no resources and no technology and no status and no power.
amy_cuddy_your_body_language_may_shape_w.zh: 沒有社會地位和權勢。把這個傳達給他們

amy_cuddy_your_body_language_may_shape_w.en: Give it to them because they can do it in private. They need their bodies, privacy and two minutes, and it can significantly change the outcomes of their life. Thank you. (Applause)
amy_cuddy_your_body_language_may_shape_w.zh: 然後這會大大地改變他們生命的結果 謝謝 (掌聲)

susan_cain_the_power_of_introverts.en: When I was nine years old, I went off to summer camp for the first time. And my mother packed me a suitcase full of books, which to me seemed like a perfectly natural thing to do.
susan_cain_the_power_of_introverts.zh: 在我九歲時 第一次要去參加夏令營，  這對我來說是完全正常的事情。

susan_cain_the_power_of_introverts.en: Because in my family, reading was the primary group activity. And this might sound antisocial to you, but for us it was really just a different way of being social.
susan_cain_the_power_of_introverts.zh: 對你來說我們似乎顯得不愛社交， 但對於我們而言，這只是另一種社交方式罷了。

susan_cain_the_power_of_introverts.en: You have the animal warmth of your family sitting right next to you, but you are also free to go roaming around the adventureland inside your own mind. And I had this idea
susan_cain_the_power_of_introverts.zh: 你能享受到家人坐在身邊 也能自由自在地  而我以為

susan_cain_the_power_of_introverts.en: that camp was going to be just like this, but better. (Laughter) I had a vision of 10 girls sitting in a cabin cozily reading books in their matching nightgowns. (Laughter)
susan_cain_the_power_of_introverts.zh: 夏令營就應該像這樣子，而且是更好一些。  我的腦海浮現著10個女孩 穿相似的睡衣，坐在小木屋裡愜意地讀書。

susan_cain_the_power_of_introverts.en: Camp was more like a keg party without any alcohol. And on the very first day, our counselor gathered us all together and she taught us a cheer that she said we would be doing
susan_cain_the_power_of_introverts.zh: 夏令營就像是一個沒有酒精飲料的派對聚會。 在入營的第一天 營長把我們召集一起 她教了我們ㄧ個在暑假餘下的日子裡

susan_cain_the_power_of_introverts.en: every day for the rest of the summer to instill camp spirit. And it went like this: "R-O-W-D-I-E, that's the way we spell rowdie. Rowdie, rowdie, let's get rowdie." (Laughter) Yeah.
susan_cain_the_power_of_introverts.zh: 這句口號是這樣的: "R-O-W-D-I-E (譯註:即 rowdy 作吵鬧解)， 我們是這樣拼rowdie的， 嘰喳嘰喳嘰，盡情吵鬧吧。"  是的。

susan_cain_the_power_of_introverts.en: So I couldn't figure out for the life of me why we were supposed to be so rowdy, or why we had to spell this word incorrectly. (Laughter)
susan_cain_the_power_of_introverts.zh: 我完全不能理解  為何要錯誤地拼這個字。 (笑聲)

susan_cain_the_power_of_introverts.en: But I recited a cheer. I recited a cheer along with everybody else. I did my best. And I just waited for the time that I could go off and read my books.
susan_cain_the_power_of_introverts.zh: 不過我還是背誦了這口號，跟其他人一同呼叫。  然後等待，

susan_cain_the_power_of_introverts.en: But the first time that I took my book out of my suitcase, the coolest girl in the bunk came up to me and she asked me, "Why are you being so mellow?" -- mellow, of course, being the exact opposite
susan_cain_the_power_of_introverts.zh: 但當我第一次從手提箱裡拿出書時，舖位上最酷的女孩走過來問我：“你為什麼這麼溫柔？” ——當然，柔和，恰恰相反

susan_cain_the_power_of_introverts.en: of R-O-W-D-I-E. And then the second time I tried it, the counselor came up to me with a concerned expression on her face and she repeated the point about camp spirit
susan_cain_the_power_of_introverts.zh: R-O-W-D-I-E。然後當我第二次嘗試時，輔導員走到我面前，臉上帶著關切的表情，她重複了關於營地精神的觀點

susan_cain_the_power_of_introverts.en: and said we should all work very hard to be outgoing. And so I put my books away, back in their suitcase, and I put them under my bed, and there they stayed for the rest of the summer.
susan_cain_the_power_of_introverts.zh: 我的行李箱裡， 把它們放在床下， 讓它整個夏天都待在那裡，

susan_cain_the_power_of_introverts.en: And I felt kind of guilty about this. I felt as if the books needed me somehow, and they were calling out to me and I was forsaking them. But I did forsake them and I didn't open that suitcase again
susan_cain_the_power_of_introverts.zh: 但我把它拋棄了而且再也沒有打開我的行李箱，

susan_cain_the_power_of_introverts.en: until I was back home with my family at the end of the summer. Now, I tell you this story about summer camp. I could have told you 50 others just like it -- all the times that I got the message
susan_cain_the_power_of_introverts.zh: 我說的這個夏令營的故事， 事實上可以有五十個相同的版本-- 旁人傳遞來的訊息總是說，

susan_cain_the_power_of_introverts.en: that somehow my quiet and introverted style of being was not necessarily the right way to go, that I should be trying to pass as more of an extrovert. And I always sensed deep down that this was wrong
susan_cain_the_power_of_introverts.zh: 我的安靜內向 不是一般人應該有的表現， 我應該更活潑外向一點。 但我內心深處我知道，

susan_cain_the_power_of_introverts.en: and that introverts were pretty excellent just as they were. But for years I denied this intuition, and so I became a Wall Street lawyer, of all things,
susan_cain_the_power_of_introverts.zh: 內向才是我真正自在的表現。  所以我成為華爾街的律師，

susan_cain_the_power_of_introverts.en: instead of the writer that I had always longed to be -- partly because I needed to prove to myself that I could be bold and assertive too. And I was always going off to crowded bars
susan_cain_the_power_of_introverts.zh: 而不是我一直想成為的作家-- 一部份原因是我想證明 我常去最熱門的酒吧，

susan_cain_the_power_of_introverts.en: when I really would have preferred to just have a nice dinner with friends. And I made these self-negating choices so reflexively, that I wasn't even aware that I was making them.
susan_cain_the_power_of_introverts.zh: 而我竟已渾然不覺。

susan_cain_the_power_of_introverts.en: Now this is what many introverts do, and it's our loss for sure, but it is also our colleagues' loss and our communities' loss. And at the risk of sounding grandiose, it is the world's loss.
susan_cain_the_power_of_introverts.zh: 更是我們同僚， 與社會群眾的損失。 聽起來言重，但這也是對這個世界的損失。

susan_cain_the_power_of_introverts.en: Because when it comes to creativity and to leadership, we need introverts doing what they do best. A third to a half of the population are introverts -- a third to a half.
susan_cain_the_power_of_introverts.zh: 因為創造力和領導力 正需要這些內斂的人發揮他們的長才。 有1/3至一半的人口是偏內向的--

susan_cain_the_power_of_introverts.en: So that's one out of every two or three people you know. So even if you're an extrovert yourself, I'm talking about your coworkers and your spouses and your children
susan_cain_the_power_of_introverts.zh: 也就是說每兩三個人中就有一個內向人口。 就算你本身是外向的， 你的同事 你的另一半和你的小孩，

susan_cain_the_power_of_introverts.en: and the person sitting next to you right now -- all of them subject to this bias that is pretty deep and real in our society. We all internalize it from a very early age
susan_cain_the_power_of_introverts.zh: 還有你旁邊坐的那位-- 都可能是受害者。 這種觀念深植在我們社會中。

susan_cain_the_power_of_introverts.en: without even having a language for what we're doing. Now, to see the bias clearly, you need to understand what introversion is. It's different from being shy. Shyness is about fear of social judgment.
susan_cain_the_power_of_introverts.zh: 就已被這種思想內化了。 為了要更加明白這個偏見， 我們必須瞭解什麼是內向。 內向不是害羞， 害羞是無法承受社會眼光。

susan_cain_the_power_of_introverts.en: Introversion is more about, how do you respond to stimulation, including social stimulation. So extroverts really crave large amounts of stimulation, whereas introverts feel at their most alive
susan_cain_the_power_of_introverts.zh: 內向比較像是 你對外在世界的反應。 外向的人需要很多的刺激， 但內斂的人相反， 他們對自身感受敏銳，

susan_cain_the_power_of_introverts.en: and their most switched-on and their most capable when they're in quieter, more low-key environments. Not all the time -- these things aren't absolute -- but a lot of the time.
susan_cain_the_power_of_introverts.zh: 當他們處於更安靜、更低調的環境時，他們的活力最高、能力最強。並非所有時候——這些事情都不是絕對的——但很多時候。

susan_cain_the_power_of_introverts.en: So the key then to maximizing our talents is for us all to put ourselves in the zone of stimulation that is right for us. But now here's where the bias comes in. Our most important institutions,
susan_cain_the_power_of_introverts.zh: 適才所長， 各司其職。 但就因為社會的偏見， 我們最重要的機構

susan_cain_the_power_of_introverts.en: our schools and our workplaces, they are designed mostly for extroverts and for extroverts' need for lots of stimulation. And also we have this belief system right now that I call the new groupthink,
susan_cain_the_power_of_introverts.zh: 學校及職場， 卻像是為外向者設計的， 提供很多的刺激給每個人。 這樣的不成文社會慣例， 我稱之為新團體思考，

susan_cain_the_power_of_introverts.en: which holds that all creativity and all productivity comes from a very oddly gregarious place. So if you picture the typical classroom nowadays: When I was going to school, we sat in rows.
susan_cain_the_power_of_introverts.zh: 把所有的想像力跟創造力 綑在一個群聚的團體中。 所以，想像一個早期典型的教室：

susan_cain_the_power_of_introverts.en: We sat in rows of desks like this, and we did most of our work pretty autonomously. But nowadays, your typical classroom has pods of desks -- four or five or six or seven kids all facing each other.
susan_cain_the_power_of_introverts.zh: 行列整齊的書桌前， 各自做著我們的功課。  四，五，六，七個小朋友面對面，

susan_cain_the_power_of_introverts.en: And kids are working in countless group assignments. Even in subjects like math and creative writing, which you think would depend on solo flights of thought,
susan_cain_the_power_of_introverts.zh: 每個人都要參與團體作業，  這種可以獨自完成的作業，

susan_cain_the_power_of_introverts.en: kids are now expected to act as committee members. And for the kids who prefer to go off by themselves or just to work alone, those kids are seen as outliers often or, worse, as problem cases.
susan_cain_the_power_of_introverts.zh: 都要小朋友像委員會成員一樣參與討論。 而那些想要獨自

susan_cain_the_power_of_introverts.en: And the vast majority of teachers reports believing that the ideal student is an extrovert as opposed to an introvert, even though introverts actually get better grades and are more knowledgeable,
susan_cain_the_power_of_introverts.zh: 雖然內向的孩子成績較好

susan_cain_the_power_of_introverts.en: according to research. (Laughter) Okay, same thing is true in our workplaces. Now, most of us work in open plan offices, without walls,
susan_cain_the_power_of_introverts.zh: 這是根據研究論文的喔。 (笑聲) 在職場上何嘗不是如此， 我們大多在開放的空間工作， 沒有隔閡，

susan_cain_the_power_of_introverts.en: where we are subject to the constant noise and gaze of our coworkers. And when it comes to leadership, introverts are routinely passed over for leadership positions,
susan_cain_the_power_of_introverts.zh: 我們持續暴露於  內向的人大多不被認為具有領導能力，

susan_cain_the_power_of_introverts.en: even though introverts tend to be very careful, much less likely to take outsize risks -- which is something we might all favor nowadays. And interesting research by Adam Grant at the Wharton School
susan_cain_the_power_of_introverts.zh: Adam Grant研究發現，

susan_cain_the_power_of_introverts.en: has found that introverted leaders often deliver better outcomes than extroverts do, because when they are managing proactive employees,
susan_cain_the_power_of_introverts.zh: 這些內向的領導者， 往往更能勝任領導職責，

susan_cain_the_power_of_introverts.en: they're much more likely to let those employees run with their ideas, whereas an extrovert can, quite unwittingly, get so excited about things that they're putting their own stamp on things,
susan_cain_the_power_of_introverts.zh: 讓有遠見的員工自由發揮，   他們的見解較為主觀，

susan_cain_the_power_of_introverts.en: and other people's ideas might not as easily then bubble up to the surface. Now in fact, some of our transformative leaders in history have been introverts. I'll give you some examples.
susan_cain_the_power_of_introverts.zh: 事實上，很多有改革力的偉大領袖是些內向的人。

susan_cain_the_power_of_introverts.en: Eleanor Roosevelt, Rosa Parks, Gandhi -- all these people described themselves as quiet and soft-spoken and even shy. And they all took the spotlight,
susan_cain_the_power_of_introverts.zh: 內向，文靜，說話溫柔， 他們矗立在鎂光燈下，

susan_cain_the_power_of_introverts.en: even though every bone in their bodies was telling them not to. And this turns out to have a special power all its own, because people could feel that these leaders were at the helm
susan_cain_the_power_of_introverts.zh: 不是因為他們天生愛指揮，

susan_cain_the_power_of_introverts.en: not because they enjoyed directing others and not out of the pleasure of being looked at; they were there because they had no choice, because they were driven to do what they thought was right.
susan_cain_the_power_of_introverts.zh: 並不是因為他們喜歡指導別人，也不是因為他們喜歡被人注視；他們在那裡是因為他們別無選擇，因為他們被迫去做他們認為正確的事情。

susan_cain_the_power_of_introverts.en: Now I think at this point it's important for me to say that I actually love extroverts. I always like to say some of my best friends are extroverts, including my beloved husband.
susan_cain_the_power_of_introverts.zh: 我很多知心友人都是外向者， 我心愛的丈夫也是。

susan_cain_the_power_of_introverts.en: And we all fall at different points, of course, along the introvert/extrovert spectrum. Even Carl Jung, the psychologist who first popularized these terms,
susan_cain_the_power_of_introverts.zh: 內向外向就像個光譜，  心理學大師榮格如是說，

susan_cain_the_power_of_introverts.en: said that there's no such thing as a pure introvert or a pure extrovert. He said that such a man would be in a lunatic asylum, if he existed at all.
susan_cain_the_power_of_introverts.zh: 說沒有純粹的內向或純粹的外向之類的東西。他說，如果有這樣的人存在的話，他一定​​會被關進瘋人院。

susan_cain_the_power_of_introverts.en: And some people fall smack in the middle of the introvert/extrovert spectrum, and we call these people ambiverts. And I often think that they have the best of all worlds.
susan_cain_the_power_of_introverts.zh: 在這道內向外向的光譜上， 我們稱之為中間性格， 我認為他們是最值得稱讚的，

susan_cain_the_power_of_introverts.en: But many of us do recognize ourselves as one type or the other. And what I'm saying is that culturally, we need a much better balance. We need more of a yin and yang between these two types.
susan_cain_the_power_of_introverts.zh: 我想表達的是，我們的社會文化需要平衡，

susan_cain_the_power_of_introverts.en: This is especially important when it comes to creativity and to productivity, because when psychologists look at the lives of the most creative people, what they find
susan_cain_the_power_of_introverts.zh: 這點在創造力與生產力的表現上 尤其重要。  不只擅長於

susan_cain_the_power_of_introverts.en: are people who are very good at exchanging ideas and advancing ideas, but who also have a serious streak of introversion in them. And this is because solitude
susan_cain_the_power_of_introverts.zh: 交換意見，溝通，  更存有內向的特質， 偶發的孤獨感，

susan_cain_the_power_of_introverts.en: is a crucial ingredient often to creativity. So Darwin, he took long walks alone in the woods and emphatically turned down dinner-party invitations. Theodor Geisel, better known as Dr. Seuss,
susan_cain_the_power_of_introverts.zh: 會獨自在樹林間漫步， 且斷然的拒絕晚餐宴會的邀約， Theodor Geisel，也就是「蘇斯博士」，

susan_cain_the_power_of_introverts.en: he dreamed up many of his amazing creations in a lonely bell tower office that he had in the back of his house in La Jolla, California.
susan_cain_the_power_of_introverts.zh: 是在他加州拉荷亞的老家

susan_cain_the_power_of_introverts.en: And he was actually afraid to meet the young children who read his books for fear that they were expecting him this kind of jolly Santa Claus-like figure
susan_cain_the_power_of_introverts.zh: 而他其實非常害怕 因為他怕小朋友們

susan_cain_the_power_of_introverts.en: and would be disappointed with his more reserved persona. Steve Wozniak invented the first Apple computer sitting alone in his cubicle in Hewlett-Packard where he was working at the time.
susan_cain_the_power_of_introverts.zh: Steve Wozniak在惠普公司

susan_cain_the_power_of_introverts.en: And he says that he never would have become such an expert in the first place had he not been too introverted to leave the house when he was growing up. Now, of course,
susan_cain_the_power_of_introverts.zh: 他說，如果他在成長過程中不是太內向，不願離開家，他一開始就不會成為這樣的專家。現在，當然，

susan_cain_the_power_of_introverts.en: this does not mean that we should all stop collaborating -- and case in point, is Steve Wozniak famously coming together with Steve Jobs to start Apple Computer --
susan_cain_the_power_of_introverts.zh: 這絕非告訴大家我們從此不要再合作了-- 好比 Steve Wozniak和賈伯斯

susan_cain_the_power_of_introverts.en: but it does mean that solitude matters and that for some people it is the air that they breathe. And in fact, we have known for centuries about the transcendent power of solitude.
susan_cain_the_power_of_introverts.zh: 但，獨立自主是非常重要的。 對一些人來說， 事實上，幾世紀以來，

susan_cain_the_power_of_introverts.en: It's only recently that we've strangely begun to forget it. If you look at most of the world's major religions, you will find seekers -- Moses, Jesus, Buddha, Muhammad --
susan_cain_the_power_of_introverts.zh: 但直到近期我們不知怎麼遺忘了。 世界上那些偉大的宗教領袖， 你會發現這些人-- 摩西，耶稣，佛祖，穆罕默德 --

susan_cain_the_power_of_introverts.en: seekers who are going off by themselves alone to the wilderness, where they then have profound epiphanies and revelations that they then bring back to the rest of the community.
susan_cain_the_power_of_introverts.zh: 他們都遠離塵囂， 他們而後尋得啟示與頓悟，

susan_cain_the_power_of_introverts.en: So, no wilderness, no revelations. This is no surprise, though, if you look at the insights of contemporary psychology. It turns out that we can't even be in a group of people
susan_cain_the_power_of_introverts.zh: 這其實不是什麼新鮮事， 仔細的看看你周遭的人們， 我們如果不模仿彼此的言行舉止，

susan_cain_the_power_of_introverts.en: without instinctively mirroring, mimicking their opinions. Even about seemingly personal and visceral things like who you're attracted to, you will start aping the beliefs of the people around you
susan_cain_the_power_of_introverts.zh: 我們根本不會變成一個群體。 我們甚至連價值觀，  你甚至會在不知不覺中，

susan_cain_the_power_of_introverts.en: without even realizing that that's what you're doing. And groups famously follow the opinions of the most dominant or charismatic person in the room, even though there's zero correlation
susan_cain_the_power_of_introverts.zh: 開始模仿周遭人們的信仰。  追隨能言善道的角色。

susan_cain_the_power_of_introverts.en: between being the best talker and having the best ideas -- I mean zero. So -- (Laughter) You might be following the person with the best ideas, but you might not.
susan_cain_the_power_of_introverts.zh: 所以說... (笑聲) 你可能正在支持最有想法的人， 也可能不是。

susan_cain_the_power_of_introverts.en: And do you really want to leave it up to chance? Much better for everybody to go off by themselves, generate their own ideas freed from the distortions of group dynamics,
susan_cain_the_power_of_introverts.zh: 難道你真的不想有雪亮的雙眼? 我們何不用自己的雙腳走入孤獨，  不被群眾思想控制，

susan_cain_the_power_of_introverts.en: and then come together as a team to talk them through in a well-managed environment and take it from there. Now if all this is true, then why are we getting it so wrong?
susan_cain_the_power_of_introverts.zh: 在一個健全的環境討論交流， 共同創造成果。 如果這一切都是真的， 我們為何錯的一蹋糊塗?

susan_cain_the_power_of_introverts.en: Why are we setting up our schools this way, and our workplaces? And why are we making these introverts feel so guilty about wanting to just go off by themselves some of the time?
susan_cain_the_power_of_introverts.zh: 我們為何把學校跟職場架設成這樣?

susan_cain_the_power_of_introverts.en: One answer lies deep in our cultural history. Western societies, and in particular the U.S., have always favored the man of action over the "man" of contemplation. But in America's early days,
susan_cain_the_power_of_introverts.zh: 我們西方社會， 特別是在美國， 總是讚揚有行動力的人， 但在美國早期，

susan_cain_the_power_of_introverts.en: we lived in what historians call a culture of character, where we still, at that point, valued people for their inner selves and their moral rectitude.
susan_cain_the_power_of_introverts.zh: 歷史學家稱之文化性格時期， 那時人們仍尊重

susan_cain_the_power_of_introverts.en: And if you look at the self-help books from this era, they all had titles with things like "Character, the Grandest Thing in the World." And they featured role models like Abraham Lincoln,
susan_cain_the_power_of_introverts.zh: 綜觀那時的勵志書籍，   例如亞伯拉罕 林肯被群眾頌揚，

susan_cain_the_power_of_introverts.en: who was praised for being modest and unassuming. Ralph Waldo Emerson called him "A man who does not offend by superiority." But then we hit the 20th century, and we entered a new culture
susan_cain_the_power_of_introverts.zh: 因其謙遜與不裝腔作勢的性格。  "不被優越感駕馭的男人"。 然而隨著二十世紀到來， 我們進入了一個新的紀元，

susan_cain_the_power_of_introverts.en: that historians call the culture of personality. What happened is we had evolved an agricultural economy to a world of big business. And so suddenly people are moving from small towns to the cities.
susan_cain_the_power_of_introverts.zh: 歷史上稱之為文化人格時期。

susan_cain_the_power_of_introverts.en: And instead of working alongside people they've known all their lives, now they are having to prove themselves in a crowd of strangers. So, quite understandably,
susan_cain_the_power_of_introverts.zh: 所以人們不再只是跟一起長大的人共事，  因此，不難理解，

susan_cain_the_power_of_introverts.en: qualities like magnetism and charisma suddenly come to seem really important. And sure enough, the self-help books change to meet these new needs and they start to have names
susan_cain_the_power_of_introverts.zh: 具有吸引力的個人魅力 自然地，勵志書籍也改變路線了，

susan_cain_the_power_of_introverts.en: like "How to Win Friends and Influence People." And they feature as their role models really great salesmen. So that's the world we're living in today. That's our cultural inheritance.
susan_cain_the_power_of_introverts.zh: "如何贏得朋友和影響他人" 等。  這就是我們所生活的今天，

susan_cain_the_power_of_introverts.en: Now none of this is to say that social skills are unimportant, and I'm also not calling for the abolishing of teamwork at all. The same religions who send their sages off to lonely mountain tops
susan_cain_the_power_of_introverts.zh: 我所說的完全不是指 我也不是在說 相同的宗教聖賢們

susan_cain_the_power_of_introverts.en: also teach us love and trust. And the problems that we are facing today in fields like science and in economics are so vast and so complex that we are going to need armies of people coming together
susan_cain_the_power_of_introverts.zh: 而我們現今所面臨的問題， 如科學突破與經濟發展， 變得如此廣闊複雜， 我們當然需要集思廣益，

susan_cain_the_power_of_introverts.en: to solve them working together. But I am saying that the more freedom that we give introverts to be themselves, the more likely that they are
susan_cain_the_power_of_introverts.zh: 共同解決它們。但我想說的是，我們給予內向的人越多的自由，讓他們做自己，就越有可能成為自己的樣子。

susan_cain_the_power_of_introverts.en: to come up with their own unique solutions to these problems. So now I'd like to share with you what's in my suitcase today. Guess what? Books. I have a suitcase full of books.
susan_cain_the_power_of_introverts.zh: 所以我想分享給各位的是， 猜猜裡面是什麼? 書。 滿滿一袋的書。

susan_cain_the_power_of_introverts.en: Here's Margaret Atwood, "Cat's Eye." Here's a novel by Milan Kundera. And here's "The Guide for the Perplexed" by Maimonides. But these are not exactly my books. I brought these books with me
susan_cain_the_power_of_introverts.zh: 這是馬格莉特著的<貓之眼>； 米蘭 昆德拉的小說； 這是<迷途中的指南> 但其實這些書不屬於我， 我會帶這些書來，

susan_cain_the_power_of_introverts.en: because they were written by my grandfather's favorite authors. My grandfather was a rabbi and he was a widower who lived alone in a small apartment in Brooklyn
susan_cain_the_power_of_introverts.zh: 是因為這些是我祖父最喜歡的作者的作品。 我祖父是猶太教教士， 祖母過世後他獨自 住在布魯克林的一間小公寓裡。

susan_cain_the_power_of_introverts.en: that was my favorite place in the world when I was growing up, partly because it was filled with his very gentle, very courtly presence and partly because it was filled with books.
susan_cain_the_power_of_introverts.zh: 那是我小時候最喜歡的地方， 部分原因是那裏充滿祖父溫柔的氛圍，

susan_cain_the_power_of_introverts.en: I mean literally every table, every chair in this apartment had yielded its original function to now serve as a surface for swaying stacks of books. Just like the rest of my family,
susan_cain_the_power_of_introverts.zh: 那裏的每張桌子，甚至椅子， 都被成推成塔的書 給排排占滿了。 就如同我家庭的其他成員，

susan_cain_the_power_of_introverts.en: my grandfather's favorite thing to do in the whole world was to read. But he also loved his congregation, and you could feel this love in the sermons that he gave
susan_cain_the_power_of_introverts.zh: 我祖父的嗜好就是閱讀。  62年來，他每周都會在

susan_cain_the_power_of_introverts.en: every week for the 62 years that he was a rabbi. He would takes the fruits of each week's reading and he would weave these intricate tapestries of ancient and humanist thought.
susan_cain_the_power_of_introverts.zh: 猶太教佈道會上講道。 他會把他從書中吸收到的智慧， 頌揚遠古流傳的人道主義思維。

susan_cain_the_power_of_introverts.en: And people would come from all over to hear him speak. But here's the thing about my grandfather. Underneath this ceremonial role, he was really modest and really introverted --
susan_cain_the_power_of_introverts.zh: 人們會從四面八方趕來聽他演講。但這是關於我祖父的事情。在這個儀式性的角色之下，他其實很謙虛，也很內向——

susan_cain_the_power_of_introverts.en: so much so that when he delivered these sermons, he had trouble making eye contact with the very same congregation that he had been speaking to for 62 years. And even away from the podium,
susan_cain_the_power_of_introverts.zh: 他甚至緊張到不敢在佈道時 跟聽眾眼神交會。 即使他都已經在同一個佈道會

susan_cain_the_power_of_introverts.en: when you called him to say hello, he would often end the conversation prematurely for fear that he was taking up too much of your time. But when he died at the age of 94,
susan_cain_the_power_of_introverts.zh: 人們向著他打招呼時， 他會草草的結束話題，  當他在94歲那年過世時，

susan_cain_the_power_of_introverts.en: the police had to close down the streets of his neighborhood to accommodate the crowd of people who came out to mourn him. And so these days I try to learn from my grandfather's example in my own way.
susan_cain_the_power_of_introverts.zh: 交警不得不關閉許多鄰近街道， 來容納蜂擁而至

susan_cain_the_power_of_introverts.en: So I just published a book about introversion, and it took me about seven years to write. And for me, that seven years was like total bliss, because I was reading, I was writing,
susan_cain_the_power_of_introverts.zh: 這本書花了我七年的時間。  因為我得以閱讀，寫作，

susan_cain_the_power_of_introverts.en: I was thinking, I was researching. It was my version of my grandfather's hours of the day alone in his library. But now all of a sudden my job is very different,
susan_cain_the_power_of_introverts.zh: 思考，研究。 相較於祖父的閱讀與佈道， 這是我的版本，我的表達方式。 但現在我的工作變得非常棘手，

susan_cain_the_power_of_introverts.en: and my job is to be out here talking about it, talking about introversion. (Laughter) And that's a lot harder for me, because as honored as I am to be here with all of you right now,
susan_cain_the_power_of_introverts.zh: 我必須要在公開場合，在講台上， 跟你們談論何謂內向。 (笑聲) 這不是我拿手的事情， 但能站在這裡向你們說話，

susan_cain_the_power_of_introverts.en: this is not my natural milieu. So I prepared for moments like these as best I could. I spent the last year practicing public speaking every chance I could get.
susan_cain_the_power_of_introverts.zh: 是何等榮耀的事情， 所以我盡我所能， 我花了一整年的時間，

susan_cain_the_power_of_introverts.en: And I call this my "year of speaking dangerously." (Laughter) And that actually helped a lot. But I'll tell you, what helps even more is my sense, my belief, my hope that when it comes to our attitudes
susan_cain_the_power_of_introverts.zh: 我稱這段時間為 "驚險的演講之年"。 (笑聲) 這其實對我幫助很大。 但讓我獲益最多的， 是我的意識，我的信念，我的希望。

susan_cain_the_power_of_introverts.en: to introversion and to quiet and to solitude, we truly are poised on the brink on dramatic change. I mean, we are. And so I am going to leave you now
susan_cain_the_power_of_introverts.zh: 沉默、與獨立者的態度，   所以，我要呼籲在座各位

susan_cain_the_power_of_introverts.en: with three calls for action for those who share this vision. Number one: Stop the madness for constant group work. Just stop it. (Laughter) Thank you. (Applause)
susan_cain_the_power_of_introverts.zh: 如果你跟我有共鳴， 第一點， 不要再瘋狂的過群體生活。 趕緊停止吧。 (笑聲) 謝謝。 (掌聲)

susan_cain_the_power_of_introverts.en: And I want to be clear about what I'm saying, because I deeply believe our offices should be encouraging casual, chatty cafe-style types of interactions -- you know, the kind where people come together
susan_cain_the_power_of_introverts.zh: 我想要再次重申，  我們的工作環境應該鼓勵 像在喝下午茶一樣自在，

susan_cain_the_power_of_introverts.en: and serendipitously have an exchange of ideas. That is great. It's great for introverts and it's great for extroverts. But we need much more privacy and much more freedom
susan_cain_the_power_of_introverts.zh: 無論是對內向或是外向者。

susan_cain_the_power_of_introverts.en: and much more autonomy at work. School, same thing. We need to be teaching kids to work together, for sure, but we also need to be teaching them how to work on their own.
