susan_cain_the_power_of_introverts.zh: 我們要教孩子們攜手合作， 但我們也要教他們如何自主作業。

susan_cain_the_power_of_introverts.en: This is especially important for extroverted children too. They need to work on their own because that is where deep thought comes from in part. Okay, number two: Go to the wilderness.
susan_cain_the_power_of_introverts.zh: 這對外向的孩子來說也尤其重要。他們需要自己工作，因為這是深刻思考的部分來源。好吧，第二點：去荒野。

susan_cain_the_power_of_introverts.en: Be like Buddha, have your own revelations. I'm not saying that we all have to now go off and build our own cabins in the woods and never talk to each other again,
susan_cain_the_power_of_introverts.zh: 像佛祖一樣，有自己的啟示。 我不是在說 我們馬上要去蓋個山中小屋隱居起來，

susan_cain_the_power_of_introverts.en: but I am saying that we could all stand to unplug and get inside our own heads a little more often. Number three: Take a good look at what's inside your own suitcase and why you put it there.
susan_cain_the_power_of_introverts.zh: 但是呼籲大家可以除去障礙，  第三點， 好好的檢視一下你的行李箱，

susan_cain_the_power_of_introverts.en: So extroverts, maybe your suitcases are also full of books. Or maybe they're full of champagne glasses or skydiving equipment. Whatever it is, I hope you take these things out every chance you get
susan_cain_the_power_of_introverts.zh: 外向的人們， 也許你行李箱也裝滿了書。  不管是什麼，我希望你不時將它拿出來，

susan_cain_the_power_of_introverts.en: and grace us with your energy and your joy. But introverts, you being you, you probably have the impulse to guard very carefully what's inside your own suitcase. And that's okay.
susan_cain_the_power_of_introverts.zh: 與我們共同分享你的快樂與能量。 而內向的人們，當你自己就好， 你或許會害怕去跟別人分享

susan_cain_the_power_of_introverts.en: But occasionally, just occasionally, I hope you will open up your suitcases for other people to see, because the world needs you and it needs the things you carry.
susan_cain_the_power_of_introverts.zh: 但偶爾，只是偶爾， 我希望你會打開你的行李箱給他人瞧，

susan_cain_the_power_of_introverts.en: So I wish you the best of all possible journeys and the courage to speak softly. Thank you very much. (Applause) Thank you. Thank you. (Applause)
susan_cain_the_power_of_introverts.zh: 我祝福你們，能有最精彩的旅程，  謝謝。 （掌聲） 謝謝。謝謝。 （掌聲）

james_veitch_this_is_what_happens_when_y.en: A few years ago, I got one of those spam emails. And it managed to get through my spam filter. I'm not quite sure how, but it turned up in my inbox, and it was from a guy called Solomon Odonkoh.
james_veitch_this_is_what_happens_when_y.zh: 幾年前， 我收到一封垃圾郵件。 它突破了我的垃圾郵件過濾器。 我不太確定它怎麼辦到的， 反正就是出現在我信箱， 寄件人是一個叫 「所羅門．沃當克」的傢伙。

james_veitch_this_is_what_happens_when_y.en: (Laughter) I know. (Laughter) It went like this: it said, "Hello James Veitch, I have an interesting business proposal I want to share with you, Solomon."
james_veitch_this_is_what_happens_when_y.zh: （笑聲）  （笑聲） 內容如下： “ 哈囉，詹姆士．維奇， 我想跟你合夥一筆有趣的生意企劃， 所羅門　敬上 "

james_veitch_this_is_what_happens_when_y.en: Now, my hand was kind of hovering on the delete button, right? I was looking at my phone. I thought, I could just delete this. Or I could do what I think we've all always wanted to do. (Laughter)
james_veitch_this_is_what_happens_when_y.zh: 我看著我的手機，我在想， 我可以就這樣把它刪掉。 或者，我可以做些我認為大家 一直都很想做的事。

james_veitch_this_is_what_happens_when_y.en: And I said, "Solomon, Your email intrigues me." (Laughter) (Applause) And the game was afoot. He said, "Dear James Veitch, We shall be shipping Gold to you." (Laughter)
james_veitch_this_is_what_happens_when_y.zh: 遊戲就此開始，

james_veitch_this_is_what_happens_when_y.en: "You will earn 10% of any gold you distributes." (Laughter) So I knew I was dealing with a professional. (Laughter) I said, "How much is it worth?" He said, "We will start with smaller quantity," --
james_veitch_this_is_what_happens_when_y.zh: 「你將獲得你所分配的黃金的 10%。」 （笑聲）所以我知道我正在和一個專業人士打交道。 （笑聲）我說：「值多少錢？」他說，「我們將從較小的數量開始，」——

james_veitch_this_is_what_happens_when_y.en: I was like, aww -- and then he said, "of 25 kgs. (Laughter) The worth should be about $2.5 million." I said, "Solomon, if we're going to do it, let's go big. (Applause)
james_veitch_this_is_what_happens_when_y.zh: 我當下就 “蛤...” 然後他說，" 25公斤 " （笑聲）

james_veitch_this_is_what_happens_when_y.en: I can handle it. How much gold do you have?" (Laughter) He said, "It is not a matter of how much gold I have, what matters is your capability of handling. We can start with 50 kgs as trial shipment."
james_veitch_this_is_what_happens_when_y.zh: （笑聲）

james_veitch_this_is_what_happens_when_y.en: I said, "50 kgs? There's no point doing this at all unless you're shipping at least a metric ton." (Laughter) (Applause) He said, "What do you do for a living?" (Laughter)
james_veitch_this_is_what_happens_when_y.zh: 我說，" 50公斤？ 這沒甚麼搞頭啊 ... 除非 ...

james_veitch_this_is_what_happens_when_y.en: I said, "I'm a hedge fund executive bank manager." (Laughter) This isn't the first time I've shipped bullion, my friend, no no no. Then I started to panic. I was like, "Where are you based?"
james_veitch_this_is_what_happens_when_y.zh: 我說，" 你們公司在哪? "

james_veitch_this_is_what_happens_when_y.en: I don't know about you, but I think if we're going via the postal service, it ought to be signed for. That's a lot of gold." He said, "It will not be easy to convince my company
james_veitch_this_is_what_happens_when_y.zh: 但我認為如果我們要透過郵寄服務，  這會有很多黃金"

james_veitch_this_is_what_happens_when_y.en: to do larger quantity shipment." I said, "Solomon, I'm completely with you on this one. I'm putting together a visual for you to take into the board meeting. Hold tight." (Laughter)
james_veitch_this_is_what_happens_when_y.zh: 我說，" 所羅門，我完全同意你這一點， 我等一下傳個圖表給你， 讓你在董事會議上說明 等我一下... "

james_veitch_this_is_what_happens_when_y.en: This is what I sent Solomon. (Laughter) (Applause) I don't know if we have any statisticians in the house, but there's definitely something going on. (Laughter)
james_veitch_this_is_what_happens_when_y.zh: 但一定是有事發生就對了。

james_veitch_this_is_what_happens_when_y.en: I said, "Solomon, attached to this email you'll find a helpful chart. I've had one of my assistants run the numbers. (Laughter) We're ready for shipping as much gold as possible."
james_veitch_this_is_what_happens_when_y.zh: 我們已準備好 盡可能多運一些黃金 "

james_veitch_this_is_what_happens_when_y.en: There's always a moment where they try to tug your heartstrings, and this was it for Solomon. He said, "I will be so much happy if the deal goes well,
james_veitch_this_is_what_happens_when_y.zh: 他說，“如果真的成交，我會非常開心，

james_veitch_this_is_what_happens_when_y.en: because I'm going to get a very good commission as well." And I said, "That's amazing, What are you going to spend your cut on?" And he said, "On RealEstate, what about you?"
james_veitch_this_is_what_happens_when_y.zh: 因為我也會得到很好的佣金。 」我說，「太棒了，你打算把你的提成花在什麼地方？他說，「在房地產方面，你呢？ 」

james_veitch_this_is_what_happens_when_y.en: I thought about it for a long time. And I said, "One word; Hummus." (Laughter) "It's going places. (Laughter) I was in Sainsbury's the other day and there were like 30 different varieties.
james_veitch_this_is_what_happens_when_y.zh: 我想了很久...     （笑聲）  那裏有30種不同的口味 ...

james_veitch_this_is_what_happens_when_y.en: Also you can cut up carrots, and you can dip them. Have you ever done that, Solomon?" (Laughter) He said, "I have to go bed now." (Laughter) (Applause) "Till morrow. Have sweet dream."
james_veitch_this_is_what_happens_when_y.zh: 你還可以切些紅蘿蔔， 沾一點鹰嘴豆沙來吃呢。 你有試過嗎，所羅門？ " （笑聲）   （掌聲） " 明天見！

james_veitch_this_is_what_happens_when_y.en: I didn't know what to say! I said, "Bonsoir my golden nugget, bonsoir." (Laughter) Guys, you have to understand, this had been going for, like, weeks, albeit hitherto the greatest weeks of my life,
james_veitch_this_is_what_happens_when_y.zh: 我說，"晚安，我的金條，晚安 "

james_veitch_this_is_what_happens_when_y.en: but I had to knock it on the head. It was getting a bit out of hand. Friends were saying, "James, do you want to come for a drink?" I was like, "I can't, I'm expecting an email about some gold."
james_veitch_this_is_what_happens_when_y.zh: 但我不得不敲它的頭。事情有點失控了。朋友們說：“詹姆斯，你想來喝一杯嗎？”我當時想，“我不能，我在等一封關於黃金的電子郵件。”

james_veitch_this_is_what_happens_when_y.en: So I figured I had to knock it on the head. I had to take it to a ridiculous conclusion. So I concocted a plan. I said, "Solomon, I'm concerned about security. When we email each other,
james_veitch_this_is_what_happens_when_y.zh: 所以我想我必須敲它的頭。我不得不得出一個荒謬的結論。所以我制定了一個計劃。我說：「所羅門，我擔心安全問題。當我們互相發送電子郵件時，

james_veitch_this_is_what_happens_when_y.en: we need to use a code." And he agreed. (Laughter) I said, "Solomon, I spent all night coming up with this code we need to use in all further correspondence: Lawyer: Gummy Bear. Bank: Cream Egg.
james_veitch_this_is_what_happens_when_y.zh: 他同意了。  我說，" 所羅門， 我花了一整晚，想到了這些暗號

james_veitch_this_is_what_happens_when_y.en: Legal: Fizzy Cola Bottle. Claim: Peanut M&Ms. Documents: Jelly Beans. Western Union: A Giant Gummy Lizard." (Laughter) I knew these were all words they use, right?
james_veitch_this_is_what_happens_when_y.zh: 法律：可樂瓶糖果， 主張聲明：M&M巧克力

james_veitch_this_is_what_happens_when_y.en: I said, "Please call me Kitkat in all further correspondence." (Laughter) I didn't hear back. I thought, I've gone too far. I've gone too far. So I had to backpedal a little.
james_veitch_this_is_what_happens_when_y.zh: 我說，" 接下來的通信， 請稱呼我 Kitkat "( 巧克力棒品牌 )   所以，我必須回頭一點點，

james_veitch_this_is_what_happens_when_y.en: I said, "Solomon, Is the deal still on? KitKat." (Laughter) Because you have to be consistent. Then I did get an email back from him. He said, "The Business is on and I am trying to blah blah blah ..."
james_veitch_this_is_what_happens_when_y.zh: 我說，" 所羅門， 我們的交易還在進行嗎？   因為你必須堅持。  他說，" 交易是還有的， 我正在...叭喇叭喇叭喇... "

james_veitch_this_is_what_happens_when_y.en: I said, "Dude, you have to use the code!" What followed is the greatest email I've ever received. (Laughter) I'm not joking, this is what turned up in my inbox. This was a good day.
james_veitch_this_is_what_happens_when_y.zh: 我說：「夥計，你必須使用密碼！」接下來是我收到過的最棒的電子郵件。 （笑聲）我不是開玩笑，這就是我收件匣中出現的內容。這是美好的一天。

james_veitch_this_is_what_happens_when_y.en: "The business is on. I am trying to raise the balance for the Gummy Bear -- (Laughter) so he can submit all the needed Fizzy Cola Bottle Jelly Beans to the Creme Egg,
james_veitch_this_is_what_happens_when_y.zh: 「生意開始了。我正在努力增加小熊軟糖的餘額——（笑聲）這樣他就可以將所有需要的碳酸可樂瓶果凍豆提交給奶油蛋，

james_veitch_this_is_what_happens_when_y.en: for the Peanut M&Ms process to start. (Laughter) Send 1,500 pounds via a Giant Gummy Lizard." (Applause) And that was so much fun, right, that it got me thinking:
james_veitch_this_is_what_happens_when_y.zh: （笑聲）     這讓我思考：

james_veitch_this_is_what_happens_when_y.en: like, what would happen if I just spent as much time as could replying to as many scam emails as I could? And that's what I've been doing for three years on your behalf. (Laughter) (Applause)
james_veitch_this_is_what_happens_when_y.zh: 如果我盡全力回這些詐騙信件 ，會發生甚麼事？    我代表各位回信給詐騙集團....

james_veitch_this_is_what_happens_when_y.en: Crazy stuff happens when you start replying to scam emails. It's really difficult, and I highly recommend we do it. I don't think what I'm doing is mean.
james_veitch_this_is_what_happens_when_y.zh: 這真的很困難， 而我強力建議大家這樣做。 我不認為這是傷天害理的，

james_veitch_this_is_what_happens_when_y.en: There are a lot of people who do mean things to scammers. All I'm doing is wasting their time. And I think any time they're spending with me is time they're not spending scamming vulnerable adults
james_veitch_this_is_what_happens_when_y.zh: 有很多人對詐騙集團很差， 而我只是在浪費他們的時間，

james_veitch_this_is_what_happens_when_y.en: out of their savings, right? And if you're going to do this -- and I highly recommend you do -- get yourself a pseudonymous email address. Don't use your own email address.
james_veitch_this_is_what_happens_when_y.zh: 先幫自己申請一個匿名的電子信箱。 不要使用自己的電子信箱，

james_veitch_this_is_what_happens_when_y.en: That's what I was doing at the start and it was a nightmare. I'd wake up in the morning and have a thousand emails about penis enlargements, only one of which was a legitimate response -- (Laughter)
james_veitch_this_is_what_happens_when_y.zh: 我一開始就是用自己的信箱， 那簡直是一場惡夢。

james_veitch_this_is_what_happens_when_y.en: to a medical question I had. But I'll tell you what, though, guys, I'll tell you what: any day is a good day, any day is a good day if you receive an email that begins like this: (Laughter)
james_veitch_this_is_what_happens_when_y.zh: 但，我要告訴各位，雖然，各位 我要告訴各位：天天都是好天 如果你收到這樣開頭的電子郵件 （笑聲）

james_veitch_this_is_what_happens_when_y.en: "I AM WINNIE MANDELA, THE SECOND WIFE OF NELSON MANDELA THE FORMER SOUTH AFRICAN PRESIDENT." I was like, oh! -- that Winnie Mandela. (Laughter) I know so many.
james_veitch_this_is_what_happens_when_y.zh: " 我是溫妮．曼德拉， 前南非總統尼爾森．曼德拉的 第二任太太 " 喔！那個溫妮．曼德拉啊， （笑聲） 我認識很多個。

james_veitch_this_is_what_happens_when_y.en: "I NEED TO TRANSFER 45 MILLION DOLLARS OUT OF THE COUNTRY BECAUSE OF MY HUSBAND NELSON MANDELA'S HEALTH CONDITION." Let that sink in. She sent me this, which is hysterical. (Laughter) And this.
james_veitch_this_is_what_happens_when_y.zh: 因為我先生的健康狀況 "  她寄給我的這個真的很搞笑，  還有這個，

james_veitch_this_is_what_happens_when_y.en: And this looks fairly legitimate, this is a letter of authorization. But to be honest, if there's nothing written on it, it's just a shape! (Laughter) I said, "Winnie, I'm really sorry to hear of this.
james_veitch_this_is_what_happens_when_y.zh: 這個看起來相當正常， 這是一封授權信，

james_veitch_this_is_what_happens_when_y.en: Given that Nelson died three months ago, I'd describe his health condition as fairly serious." (Laughter) That's the worst health condition you can have, not being alive.
james_veitch_this_is_what_happens_when_y.zh: 鑒於尼爾森死了三個月，

james_veitch_this_is_what_happens_when_y.en: She said, "KINDLY COMPLY WITH MY BANKERS INSTRUCTIONS. ONE LOVE." (Laughter) I said, "Of course. NO WOMAN, NO CRY." (Laughter) (Applause)
james_veitch_this_is_what_happens_when_y.zh: 唯一的愛 "

james_veitch_this_is_what_happens_when_y.en: She said, "MY BANKER WILL NEED TRANSFER OF 3000 DOLLARS. ONE LOVE." (Laughter) I said, "no problemo. I SHOT THE SHERIFF." [ (BUT I DID NOT SHOOT THE DEPUTY) ] (Laughter) Thank you. (Applause)
james_veitch_this_is_what_happens_when_y.zh: （笑聲）  我幫你幹掉司法官了"  （笑聲） 謝謝 （掌聲）

julian_treasure_how_to_speak_so_that_peo.en: The human voice: It's the instrument we all play. It's the most powerful sound in the world, probably. It's the only one that can start a war or say "I love you."
julian_treasure_how_to_speak_so_that_peo.zh: 我們每個人， 都會運用語音。

julian_treasure_how_to_speak_so_that_peo.en: And yet many people have the experience that when they speak, people don't listen to them. And why is that? How can we speak powerfully to make change in the world? What I'd like to suggest,
julian_treasure_how_to_speak_so_that_peo.zh: 很多人都有相似的經歷：  這是為什麼呢？ 如何讓話語擲地有聲，

julian_treasure_how_to_speak_so_that_peo.en: there are a number of habits that we need to move away from. I've assembled for your pleasure here seven deadly sins of speaking. I'm not pretending this is an exhaustive list,
julian_treasure_how_to_speak_so_that_peo.zh: 內容並非盡善盡美，

julian_treasure_how_to_speak_so_that_peo.en: but these seven, I think, are pretty large habits that we can all fall into. First, gossip. Speaking ill of somebody who's not present. Not a nice habit, and we know perfectly well
julian_treasure_how_to_speak_so_that_peo.zh: 但是這七條過失， 首先，「說長道短」。 我們在別人背後指指點點， 這是個壞習慣。

julian_treasure_how_to_speak_so_that_peo.en: the person gossiping, five minutes later, will be gossiping about us. Second, judging. We know people who are like this in conversation, and it's very hard to listen to somebody
julian_treasure_how_to_speak_so_that_peo.zh: 第二，「評斷」。 很多人在交談時都很喜歡評判。

julian_treasure_how_to_speak_so_that_peo.en: if you know that you're being judged and found wanting at the same time. Third, negativity. You can fall into this. My mother, in the last years of her life, became very negative,
julian_treasure_how_to_speak_so_that_peo.zh: 第三，「心態消極」。

julian_treasure_how_to_speak_so_that_peo.en: and it's hard to listen. I remember one day, I said to her, "It's October 1 today," and she said, "I know, isn't it dreadful?" (Laughter) It's hard to listen when somebody's that negative. (Laughter)
julian_treasure_how_to_speak_so_that_peo.zh: 大家可不想聽那麼消極的人說話。

julian_treasure_how_to_speak_so_that_peo.en: And another form of negativity, complaining. Well, this is the national art of the U.K. It's our national sport. We complain about the weather, sport, about politics, about everything,
julian_treasure_how_to_speak_so_that_peo.zh: 這是全民運動，我們抱怨天氣、

julian_treasure_how_to_speak_so_that_peo.en: but actually, complaining is viral misery. It's not spreading sunshine and lightness in the world. Excuses. We've all met this guy. Maybe we've all been this guy. Some people have a blamethrower.
julian_treasure_how_to_speak_so_that_peo.zh: 「找藉口」。我們都碰過這種人。

julian_treasure_how_to_speak_so_that_peo.en: They just pass it on to everybody else and don't take responsibility for their actions, and again, hard to listen to somebody who is being like that. Penultimate, the sixth of the seven,
julian_treasure_how_to_speak_so_that_peo.zh: 他們推諉塞責， 不為自己的言行承擔責任，

julian_treasure_how_to_speak_so_that_peo.en: embroidery, exaggeration. It demeans our language, actually, sometimes. For example, if I see something that really is awesome, what do I call it? (Laughter)
julian_treasure_how_to_speak_so_that_peo.zh: 這可以說是對語言的褻瀆。 假如一個說話誇張的人， 那麼他還能用什麼形容詞呢？

julian_treasure_how_to_speak_so_that_peo.en: And then, of course, this exaggeration becomes lying, and we don't want to listen to people we know are lying to us. And finally, dogmatism. The confusion of facts with opinions.
julian_treasure_how_to_speak_so_that_peo.zh: 其實，語言過分誇張就是欺騙，

julian_treasure_how_to_speak_so_that_peo.en: When those two things get conflated, you're listening into the wind. You know, somebody is bombarding you with their opinions as if they were true. It's difficult to listen to that.
julian_treasure_how_to_speak_so_that_peo.zh: 這樣講話只會成為別人的耳旁風。

julian_treasure_how_to_speak_so_that_peo.en: So here they are, seven deadly sins of speaking. These are things I think we need to avoid. But is there a positive way to think about this? Yes, there is.
julian_treasure_how_to_speak_so_that_peo.zh: 以上就是講話中的「七宗罪」。 我們應該避免以上的錯誤。 那麼在講話時是否有能遵循的原則呢？ 是有幾項。

julian_treasure_how_to_speak_so_that_peo.en: I'd like to suggest that there are four really powerful cornerstones, foundations, that we can stand on if we want our speech to be powerful and to make change in the world.
julian_treasure_how_to_speak_so_that_peo.zh: 我想建議的是，如果我們希望我們的演講具有力量並改變世界，我們可以依靠四個真正強大的基石和基礎。

julian_treasure_how_to_speak_so_that_peo.en: Fortunately, these things spell a word. The word is "hail," and it has a great definition as well. I'm not talking about the stuff that falls from the sky and hits you on the head.
julian_treasure_how_to_speak_so_that_peo.zh: 這就是「HAIL」，這個單詞 本身就包含深刻的意義。 我指的不是從天而下、

julian_treasure_how_to_speak_so_that_peo.en: I'm talking about this definition, to greet or acclaim enthusiastically, which is how I think our words will be received if we stand on these four things. So what do they stand for?
julian_treasure_how_to_speak_so_that_peo.zh: 我說的這個定義是熱情地打招呼或喝采，我想如果我們站在這四件事上，我們的話就會被接受。那麼它們代表什麼？

julian_treasure_how_to_speak_so_that_peo.en: See if you can guess. The H, honesty, of course, being true in what you say, being straight and clear. The A is authenticity, just being yourself.
julian_treasure_how_to_speak_so_that_peo.zh: 你猜猜看。  講話的內容要真實，且清晰無任何歪曲。 「A」代表內心誠懇、 正心誠意（Authenticity）。

julian_treasure_how_to_speak_so_that_peo.en: A friend of mine described it as standing in your own truth, which I think is a lovely way to put it. The I is integrity, being your word, actually doing what you say,
julian_treasure_how_to_speak_so_that_peo.zh: 我朋友認為， 這個解釋非常到位。 「I」代表言行一致（Integrity）， 不能說一套做一套，

julian_treasure_how_to_speak_so_that_peo.en: and being somebody people can trust. And the L is love. I don't mean romantic love, but I do mean wishing people well, for two reasons. First of all, I think absolute honesty may not be what we want.
julian_treasure_how_to_speak_so_that_peo.zh: 做一個能讓人信任的人。  這不是指男女之間的愛情， 而是希望幫助別人、成就別人。

julian_treasure_how_to_speak_so_that_peo.en: I mean, my goodness, you look ugly this morning. Perhaps that's not necessary. Tempered with love, of course, honesty is a great thing. But also, if you're really wishing somebody well,
julian_treasure_how_to_speak_so_that_peo.zh: 是沒有必要的。 如果你內心充滿慈愛， 那麼誠實就是很大的優點。 如果你真心希望別人受益，

julian_treasure_how_to_speak_so_that_peo.en: it's very hard to judge them at the same time. I'm not even sure you can do those two things simultaneously. So hail. Also, now that's what you say, and it's like the old song, it is what you say,
julian_treasure_how_to_speak_so_that_peo.zh: 你就不會指摘別人的不是， 因為你無法同時做到這兩件事。  以上介紹的是講話的內容， 就像一首老歌裡唱的，你說的話重要，

julian_treasure_how_to_speak_so_that_peo.en: it's also the way that you say it. You have an amazing toolbox. This instrument is incredible, and yet this is a toolbox that very few people have ever opened.
julian_treasure_how_to_speak_so_that_peo.zh: 寶庫裡藏著十分有用的工具， 但很少有人開啟這座寶庫。

julian_treasure_how_to_speak_so_that_peo.en: I'd like to have a little rummage in there with you now and just pull a few tools out that you might like to take away and play with, which will increase the power of your speaking.
julian_treasure_how_to_speak_so_that_peo.zh: 接下來我將與各位一起進入寶庫探索，

julian_treasure_how_to_speak_so_that_peo.en: Register, for example. Now, falsetto register may not be very useful most of the time, but there's a register in between. I'm not going to get very technical about this
julian_treasure_how_to_speak_so_that_peo.zh: 在講話時，假音往往不太有效， 因此我們應當使用較為適中的聲區。 我不想說得太專業，以免班門弄斧，

julian_treasure_how_to_speak_so_that_peo.en: for any of you who are voice coaches. You can locate your voice, however. So if I talk up here in my nose, you can hear the difference. If I go down here in my throat,
julian_treasure_how_to_speak_so_that_peo.zh: 對於你們中的任何一位語音教練來說。不過，您可以找到您的聲音。所以如果我用鼻子說話，你就能聽出其中的差異。如果我進入喉嚨，

julian_treasure_how_to_speak_so_that_peo.en: which is where most of us speak from most of the time. But if you want weight, you need to go down here to the chest. You hear the difference? We vote for politicians with lower voices, it's true,
julian_treasure_how_to_speak_so_that_peo.zh: 就可以透過胸腔發聲。 你能聽出區別嗎？ 我們會把票投給嗓音低沉的候選人，

julian_treasure_how_to_speak_so_that_peo.en: because we associate depth with power and with authority. That's register. Then we have timbre. It's the way your voice feels. Again, the research shows
julian_treasure_how_to_speak_so_that_peo.zh: 這是「聲區」。 下面我們介紹「音色」， 也就是嗓音帶給別人的感覺。 研究顯示，我們偏好

julian_treasure_how_to_speak_so_that_peo.en: that we prefer voices which are rich, smooth, warm, like hot chocolate. Well if that's not you, that's not the end of the world, because you can train. Go and get a voice coach.
julian_treasure_how_to_speak_so_that_peo.zh: 你沒有這種嗓音也不要緊， 可以透過訓練改變， 可以尋求聲樂導師的幫助。

julian_treasure_how_to_speak_so_that_peo.en: And there are amazing things you can do with breathing, with posture, and with exercises to improve the timbre of your voice. Then prosody. I love prosody. This is the sing-song, the meta-language
julian_treasure_how_to_speak_so_that_peo.zh: 你也可以在很多方面下功夫，  這樣你就能改善自己的音色。 第三是注意「抑揚頓挫」。 這就好比是歌唱，

julian_treasure_how_to_speak_so_that_peo.en: that we use in order to impart meaning. It's root one for meaning in conversation. People who speak all on one note are really quite hard to listen to if they don't have any prosody at all.
julian_treasure_how_to_speak_so_that_peo.zh: 它能使語意層次分明， 這是談話中表達意義的根本。 單一的語調 缺乏抑揚頓挫就不會收到理想效果，

julian_treasure_how_to_speak_so_that_peo.en: That's where the word "monotonic" comes from, or monotonous, monotone. Also, we have repetitive prosody now coming in, where every sentence ends as if it were a question
julian_treasure_how_to_speak_so_that_peo.zh: 這會讓世界變得 平淡而單調。 但也不要頻繁而重複地使用語調， 不要在每句話的結尾使用升調， 讓人誤以為是疑問句，

julian_treasure_how_to_speak_so_that_peo.en: when it's actually not a question, it's a statement? (Laughter) And if you repeat that one, it's actually restricting your ability to communicate through prosody, which I think is a shame,
julian_treasure_how_to_speak_so_that_peo.zh: 重複地使用語調 會侷限原本該有的溝通效果，

julian_treasure_how_to_speak_so_that_peo.en: so let's try and break that habit. Pace. I can get very excited by saying something really quickly, or I can slow right down to emphasize, and at the end of that, of course, is our old friend silence.
julian_treasure_how_to_speak_so_that_peo.zh: 接下來，我要介紹經典的一招：

julian_treasure_how_to_speak_so_that_peo.en: There's nothing wrong with a bit of silence in a talk, is there? We don't have to fill it with ums and ahs. It can be very powerful. Of course, pitch often goes along with pace
julian_treasure_how_to_speak_so_that_peo.zh: 在講話中適時地沉默片刻 為什麼要使用無謂的「嗯」和「啊」呢？  並用「音高」與「語速」時，

julian_treasure_how_to_speak_so_that_peo.en: to indicate arousal, but you can do it just with pitch. Where did you leave my keys? (Higher pitch) Where did you leave my keys? So, slightly different meaning in those two deliveries.
julian_treasure_how_to_speak_so_that_peo.zh: 往往能喚起聽者的注意， 只有音高也有效。  你把我的鑰匙放在哪兒了？

julian_treasure_how_to_speak_so_that_peo.en: And finally, volume. (Loud) I can get really excited by using volume. Sorry about that, if I startled anybody. Or, I can have you really pay attention by getting very quiet.
julian_treasure_how_to_speak_so_that_peo.zh: 最後是「音量」。 這樣大的音量讓我顯得非常興奮， 非常抱歉嚇了你們一跳。 我也可以把聲音放輕，

julian_treasure_how_to_speak_so_that_peo.en: Some people broadcast the whole time. Try not to do that. That's called sodcasting, (Laughter) Imposing your sound on people around you carelessly and inconsiderately. Not nice.
julian_treasure_how_to_speak_so_that_peo.zh: 這就像在公共場所用手機放音樂，

julian_treasure_how_to_speak_so_that_peo.en: Of course, where this all comes into play most of all is when you've got something really important to do. It might be standing on a stage like this and giving a talk to people.
julian_treasure_how_to_speak_so_that_peo.zh: 當然，這一切最重要的是當你有一些非常重要的事情要做時。它可能會站在這樣的舞台上向人們演講。

julian_treasure_how_to_speak_so_that_peo.en: It might be proposing marriage, asking for a raise, a wedding speech. Whatever it is, if it's really important, you owe it to yourself to look at this toolbox and the engine that it's going to work on,
julian_treasure_how_to_speak_so_that_peo.zh: 或者在婚禮上致賀詞等等， 這些都屬於重要場合。 大家有必要看看

julian_treasure_how_to_speak_so_that_peo.en: and no engine works well without being warmed up. Warm up your voice. Actually, let me show you how to do that. Would you all like to stand up for a moment? I'm going to show you
julian_treasure_how_to_speak_so_that_peo.zh: 但機器工作前都需要預熱， 否則就不能發揮性能。 聲音也需要熱身。   我將示範口腔體操「六部曲」。

julian_treasure_how_to_speak_so_that_peo.en: the six vocal warm-up exercises that I do before every talk I ever do. Any time you're going to talk to anybody important, do these. First, arms up, deep breath in, and sigh out, ahhhhh, like that.
julian_treasure_how_to_speak_so_that_peo.zh: 如果你要和別人談重要的事情， 就做以下練習：  然後吐氣，「呵……」就像這樣。

julian_treasure_how_to_speak_so_that_peo.en: One more time. Ahhhh, very good. Now we're going to warm up our lips, and we're going to go Ba, Ba, Ba, Ba, Ba, Ba, Ba, Ba. Very good. And now, brrrrrrrrrr, just like when you were a kid.
julian_treasure_how_to_speak_so_that_peo.zh: 再來一次。 「呵……」，非常好。 現在要鍛煉嘴脣了。 像這樣「啵、啵、啵、啵」， 「啵、啵、啵、啵」，非常好。 現在是「噗……」， 就像個小孩子一樣，

julian_treasure_how_to_speak_so_that_peo.en: Brrrr. Now your lips should be coming alive. We're going to do the tongue next with exaggerated la, la, la, la, la, la, la, la, la. Beautiful. You're getting really good at this.
julian_treasure_how_to_speak_so_that_peo.zh: 現在是舌尖上的體操。  大家都做得非常好。

julian_treasure_how_to_speak_so_that_peo.en: And then, roll an R. Rrrrrrr. That's like champagne for the tongue. Finally, and if I can only do one, the pros call this the siren. It's really good. It starts with "we" and goes to "aw."
julian_treasure_how_to_speak_so_that_peo.zh: 這是舌尖上的華爾滋。 最後一個練習，「六部曲」的壓軸戲， 專家們稱之為「誘人的歌聲」。 這是非常精彩的體操。 從「嗚伊」開始進入「噢」，

julian_treasure_how_to_speak_so_that_peo.en: The "we" is high, the "aw" is low. So you go, weeeaawww, weeeaawww. Fantastic. Give yourselves a round of applause. Take a seat, thank you. (Applause) Next time you speak, do those in advance.
julian_treasure_how_to_speak_so_that_peo.zh: 非常棒。請給自己一些掌聲吧。   下次在您講話前別忘了先做口腔體操。

julian_treasure_how_to_speak_so_that_peo.en: Now let me just put this in context to close. This is a serious point here. This is where we are now, right? We speak not very well to people who simply aren't listening
julian_treasure_how_to_speak_so_that_peo.zh: 讓我回到主題來做個總結， 我們提出了一個重要的主題。 我們現在是這樣吧？ 我們講的話， 讓別人聽不進去。

julian_treasure_how_to_speak_so_that_peo.en: in an environment that's all about noise and bad acoustics. I have talked about that on this stage in different phases. What would the world be like if we were speaking powerfully
julian_treasure_how_to_speak_so_that_peo.zh: 如果我們講的話影響力強大， 讓別人樂於傾聽，

julian_treasure_how_to_speak_so_that_peo.en: to people who were listening consciously in environments which were actually fit for purpose? Or to make that a bit larger, what would the world be like if we were creating sound consciously
julian_treasure_how_to_speak_so_that_peo.zh: 而且各種外在條件也適合我們講話，  如果擴大範圍來講， 如果我們有意識地發出聲音、

julian_treasure_how_to_speak_so_that_peo.en: and consuming sound consciously and designing all our environments consciously for sound? That would be a world that does sound beautiful, and one where understanding would be the norm,
julian_treasure_how_to_speak_so_that_peo.zh: 並且很好地設置 各種外在條件方便聲音傳播，   在這個世界中，

julian_treasure_how_to_speak_so_that_peo.en: and that is an idea worth spreading. Thank you. (Applause)
julian_treasure_how_to_speak_so_that_peo.zh: 這是個值得分享的點子。 感謝大家。

dan_pink_the_puzzle_of_motivation.en: I need to make a confession at the outset here. A little over 20 years ago, I did something that I regret, something that I'm not particularly proud of.
dan_pink_the_puzzle_of_motivation.zh: 我首先需要在這裡坦白。二十多年前，我做了一些讓我後悔的事情，一些我並不特別自豪的事。

dan_pink_the_puzzle_of_motivation.en: Something that, in many ways, I wish no one would ever know, but here I feel kind of obliged to reveal. (Laughter) In the late 1980s, in a moment of youthful indiscretion, I went to law school.
dan_pink_the_puzzle_of_motivation.zh: 從很多方面來說，我希望沒有人知道這一點，但在這裡我覺得有必要透露。 （笑聲） 20 世紀 80 年代末，在年輕時的輕率時刻，我進入了法學院。

dan_pink_the_puzzle_of_motivation.en: (Laughter) In America, law is a professional degree: after your university degree, you go on to law school. When I got to law school, I didn't do very well. To put it mildly, I didn't do very well.
dan_pink_the_puzzle_of_motivation.zh: （笑聲）在美國，法律是一個專業學位：獲得大學學位後，你可以繼續進入法學院。當我進入法學院時，我的成績並不好。溫和地說，我做得不太好。

dan_pink_the_puzzle_of_motivation.en: I, in fact, graduated in the part of my law school class that made the top 90% possible. (Laughter) Thank you. I never practiced law a day in my life; I pretty much wasn't allowed to. (Laughter)
dan_pink_the_puzzle_of_motivation.zh: (笑聲) 謝謝！

dan_pink_the_puzzle_of_motivation.en: But today, against my better judgment, against the advice of my own wife, I want to try to dust off some of those legal skills -- what's left of those legal skills. I don't want to tell you a story.
dan_pink_the_puzzle_of_motivation.zh: 把我那些封塵的法律知識拿出來， 雖然已經所餘無幾，

dan_pink_the_puzzle_of_motivation.en: I want to make a case. I want to make a hard-headed, evidence-based, dare I say lawyerly case, for rethinking how we run our businesses. So, ladies and gentlemen of the jury, take a look at this.
dan_pink_the_puzzle_of_motivation.zh: 我想作一個陳述，   一個使我們重新思考

dan_pink_the_puzzle_of_motivation.en: This is called the candle problem. Some of you might know it. It's created in 1945 by a psychologist named Karl Duncker. He created this experiment
dan_pink_the_puzzle_of_motivation.zh: 在一九四五年想出來的， 頓克這個實驗，

dan_pink_the_puzzle_of_motivation.en: that is used in many other experiments in behavioral science. And here's how it works. Suppose I'm the experimenter. I bring you into a room. I give you a candle, some thumbtacks and some matches.
dan_pink_the_puzzle_of_motivation.zh: 被廣泛的應用在各類的行為科學研究中，  我帶你進入一個房間，給你一支蠟燭，

dan_pink_the_puzzle_of_motivation.en: And I say to you, "Your job is to attach the candle to the wall so the wax doesn't drip onto the table." Now what would you do? Many people begin trying to thumbtack the candle to the wall.
dan_pink_the_puzzle_of_motivation.zh: 但蠟不可滴到桌子上。」你會怎麼做？

dan_pink_the_puzzle_of_motivation.en: Doesn't work. I saw somebody kind of make the motion over here -- some people have a great idea where they light the match, melt the side of the candle, try to adhere it to the wall.
dan_pink_the_puzzle_of_motivation.zh: 不起作用。我看到有人在這裡做出了一個動作——有些人有一個好主意，他們點燃火柴，融化蠟燭的一側，試著將它黏在牆上。

dan_pink_the_puzzle_of_motivation.en: It's an awesome idea. Doesn't work. And eventually, after five or ten minutes, most people figure out the solution, which you can see here. The key is to overcome what's called functional fixedness.
dan_pink_the_puzzle_of_motivation.zh: 很多人都找到答案了， 就是這個，

dan_pink_the_puzzle_of_motivation.en: You look at that box and you see it only as a receptacle for the tacks. But it can also have this other function, as a platform for the candle. The candle problem.
dan_pink_the_puzzle_of_motivation.zh: 你看那個盒子，你只會看到它是大頭釘的容器。但它還可以具有其他功能，作為蠟燭的平台。蠟燭問題。

dan_pink_the_puzzle_of_motivation.en: I want to tell you about an experiment using the candle problem, done by a scientist named Sam Glucksberg, who is now at Princeton University, US, This shows the power of incentives.
dan_pink_the_puzzle_of_motivation.zh: 現在我要講一個

dan_pink_the_puzzle_of_motivation.en: He gathered his participants and said: "I'm going to time you, how quickly you can solve this problem." To one group he said, "I'm going to time you to establish norms,
dan_pink_the_puzzle_of_motivation.zh: 實驗是這樣的：他找來一批參與者， 對他們說：「我會為你們計時，看看誰最快能找到問題的答案。」

dan_pink_the_puzzle_of_motivation.en: averages for how long it typically takes someone to solve this sort of problem." To the second group he offered rewards. He said, "If you're in the top 25% of the fastest times, you get five dollars.
dan_pink_the_puzzle_of_motivation.zh: 會用作建立平均數，  他說﹔「最快解難的四分一人

dan_pink_the_puzzle_of_motivation.en: If you're the fastest of everyone we're testing here today, you get 20 dollars." Now this is several years ago, adjusted for inflation, it's a decent sum of money for a few minutes of work.
dan_pink_the_puzzle_of_motivation.zh: 這個實驗在多年前進行，把通漲算在內，

dan_pink_the_puzzle_of_motivation.en: It's a nice motivator. Question: How much faster did this group solve the problem? Answer: It took them, on average, three and a half minutes longer. 3.5 min longer. This makes no sense, right?
dan_pink_the_puzzle_of_motivation.zh: 問題是：

dan_pink_the_puzzle_of_motivation.en: I mean, I'm an American. I believe in free markets. That's not how it's supposed to work, right? (Laughter) If you want people to perform better, you reward them. Right?
dan_pink_the_puzzle_of_motivation.zh: (笑聲)

dan_pink_the_puzzle_of_motivation.en: Bonuses, commissions, their own reality show. Incentivize them. That's how business works. But that's not happening here. You've got an incentive designed to sharpen thinking and accelerate creativity,
dan_pink_the_puzzle_of_motivation.zh: 都是獎勵，商業社會就是這樣運作的，    能使他們思考更快，創造力更高，

dan_pink_the_puzzle_of_motivation.en: and it does just the opposite. It dulls thinking and blocks creativity. What's interesting about this experiment is that it's not an aberration. This has been replicated over and over again
dan_pink_the_puzzle_of_motivation.zh: 思考失色了，創造力受到窒礙， 你要知道，這個實驗結果並不是偶然的偏差，

dan_pink_the_puzzle_of_motivation.en: for nearly 40 years. These contingent motivators -- if you do this, then you get that -- work in some circumstances. But for a lot of tasks, they actually either don't work or, often, they do harm.
dan_pink_the_puzzle_of_motivation.zh: 你做了這個，便得到那個，  但在很多其他事情上卻不行，

dan_pink_the_puzzle_of_motivation.en: This is one of the most robust findings in social science, and also one of the most ignored. I spent the last couple of years looking at the science of human motivation,
dan_pink_the_puzzle_of_motivation.zh: 這是社會科學中最有力的發現之一，也是最容易被忽略的發現之一。過去幾年我一直在研究人類動機的科學，

dan_pink_the_puzzle_of_motivation.en: particularly the dynamics of extrinsic motivators and intrinsic motivators. And I'm telling you, it's not even close. If you look at the science, there is a mismatch between what science knows
dan_pink_the_puzzle_of_motivation.zh: 我可以告訴你，兩者簡直南轅北轍，

dan_pink_the_puzzle_of_motivation.en: and what business does. What's alarming here is that our business operating system -- think of the set of assumptions and protocols beneath our businesses,
dan_pink_the_puzzle_of_motivation.zh: 以及做什麼業務。這裡令人擔憂的是我們的業務作業系統——想想我們業務背後的一組假設和協議，

dan_pink_the_puzzle_of_motivation.en: how we motivate people, how we apply our human resources-- it's built entirely around these extrinsic motivators, around carrots and sticks. That's actually fine for many kinds of 20th century tasks.
dan_pink_the_puzzle_of_motivation.zh: how we motivate people, how we apply our human resources-- it's built entirely around these extrinsic motivators, around carrots and sticks.對於 20 世紀的許多任務來說，這實際上已經足夠了。

dan_pink_the_puzzle_of_motivation.en: But for 21st century tasks, that mechanistic, reward-and-punishment approach doesn't work, often doesn't work, and often does harm. Let me show you. Glucksberg did another similar experiment,
dan_pink_the_puzzle_of_motivation.zh: 但對於 21 世紀的任務來說，這種機械性的獎懲方法不起作用，而且常常不起作用，而且常常有害。讓我告訴你。格魯克斯伯格做了另一個類似的實驗，

dan_pink_the_puzzle_of_motivation.en: he presented the problem in a slightly different way, like this up here. Attach the candle to the wall so the wax doesn't drip onto the table. Same deal. You: we're timing for norms.
dan_pink_the_puzzle_of_motivation.zh: 他以稍微不同的方式提出了這個問題，就像這裡的一樣。將蠟燭固定在牆上，這樣蠟就不會滴到桌子上。同樣的交易。你：我們正在製定規範。

dan_pink_the_puzzle_of_motivation.en: You: we're incentivizing. What happened this time? This time, the incentivized group kicked the other group's butt. Why? Because when the tacks are out of the box, it's pretty easy isn't it? (Laughter)
dan_pink_the_puzzle_of_motivation.zh: 你：我們正在激勵。這次發生了什麼事？這一次，受到激勵的小組打敗了另一組。為什麼？因為當圖釘開箱即用時，這很容易，不是嗎？ （笑聲）

dan_pink_the_puzzle_of_motivation.en: If-then rewards work really well for those sorts of tasks, where there is a simple set of rules and a clear destination to go to. Rewards, by their very nature, narrow our focus, concentrate the mind;
dan_pink_the_puzzle_of_motivation.zh: 獎勵的本身， 把我們的視野收窄，思想聚焦，

dan_pink_the_puzzle_of_motivation.en: that's why they work in so many cases. So, for tasks like this, a narrow focus, where you just see the goal right there, zoom straight ahead to it, they work really well.
dan_pink_the_puzzle_of_motivation.zh: 它就是這樣發揮作用，

dan_pink_the_puzzle_of_motivation.en: But for the real candle problem, you don't want to be looking like this. The solution is on the periphery. You want to be looking around. That reward actually narrows our focus
dan_pink_the_puzzle_of_motivation.zh: 但對於真正的蠟燭問題，你不希望看起來像這樣。解決方案就在外圍。你想要四處看看。這種獎勵實際上縮小了我們的注意力

dan_pink_the_puzzle_of_motivation.en: and restricts our possibility. Let me tell you why this is so important. In western Europe, in many parts of Asia, in North America, in Australia,
dan_pink_the_puzzle_of_motivation.zh: 讓我告訴你這個認知的重要性，

dan_pink_the_puzzle_of_motivation.en: white-collar workers are doing less of this kind of work, and more of this kind of work. That routine, rule-based, left-brain work -- certain kinds of accounting, financial analysis,
dan_pink_the_puzzle_of_motivation.zh: 白領做這樣的工作越來越少，越來越多做這樣的工作。例行的、基於規則的左腦工作－某些類型的會計、財務分析、

dan_pink_the_puzzle_of_motivation.en: computer programming -- has become fairly easy to outsource, fairly easy to automate. Software can do it faster. Low-cost providers can do it cheaper. So what really matters
dan_pink_the_puzzle_of_motivation.zh: 電腦程式設計－已經變得相當容易外包，相當容易自動化。軟體可以更快地做到這一點。低成本提供者可以更便宜地做到這一點。那麼真正重要的是

dan_pink_the_puzzle_of_motivation.en: are the more right-brained creative, conceptual kinds of abilities. Think about your own work. Think about your own work. Are the problems that you face,
dan_pink_the_puzzle_of_motivation.zh: 是更右腦的創造性、概念能力。想想你自己的工作。想想你自己的工作。您所面臨的問題，

dan_pink_the_puzzle_of_motivation.en: or even the problems we've been talking about here, do they have a clear set of rules, and a single solution? No. The rules are mystifying. The solution, if it exists at all,
dan_pink_the_puzzle_of_motivation.zh: 是不是那類問題？   答案，如果有的話，

dan_pink_the_puzzle_of_motivation.en: is surprising and not obvious. Everybody in this room is dealing with their own version of the candle problem. And for candle problems of any kind, in any field, those if-then rewards,
dan_pink_the_puzzle_of_motivation.zh: 在座的每一位，  而對於各式各樣的蠟燭難題，

dan_pink_the_puzzle_of_motivation.en: the things around which we've built so many of our businesses, don't work! It makes me crazy. And here's the thing. This is not a feeling. Okay? I'm a lawyer; I don't believe in feelings.
dan_pink_the_puzzle_of_motivation.zh: 我們許多企業所圍繞的東西根本不起作用！這讓我抓狂。事情是這樣的。這不是一種感覺。好的？我是律師；我不相信感情。

dan_pink_the_puzzle_of_motivation.en: This is not a philosophy. I'm an American; I don't believe in philosophy. (Laughter) This is a fact -- or, as we say in my hometown of Washington, D.C., a true fact. (Laughter) (Applause)
dan_pink_the_puzzle_of_motivation.zh: 這不是一種哲學。我是美國人；我不相信哲學。 （笑聲）這是一個事實——或者，正如我們在我的家鄉華盛頓特區所說的那樣，這是一個真實的事實。 （笑聲）（掌聲）

dan_pink_the_puzzle_of_motivation.en: Let me give you an example. Let me marshal the evidence here. I'm not telling a story, I'm making a case. Ladies and gentlemen of the jury, some evidence:
dan_pink_the_puzzle_of_motivation.zh: 因為我不是在講故事，我在陳述一個論點，

dan_pink_the_puzzle_of_motivation.en: Dan Ariely, one of the great economists of our time, he and three colleagues did a study of some MIT students. They gave these MIT students a bunch of games, games that involved creativity,
dan_pink_the_puzzle_of_motivation.zh: 需要創意、

dan_pink_the_puzzle_of_motivation.en: and motor skills, and concentration. And the offered them, for performance, three levels of rewards: small reward, medium reward, large reward. If you do really well you get the large reward, on down.
dan_pink_the_puzzle_of_motivation.zh: 靈巧和專注的遊戲，

dan_pink_the_puzzle_of_motivation.en: What happened? As long as the task involved only mechanical skill bonuses worked as they would be expected: the higher the pay, the better the performance. Okay?
dan_pink_the_puzzle_of_motivation.zh: 奬品就如所料的發揮作用， 奬勵越大，表現越好，

dan_pink_the_puzzle_of_motivation.en: But once the task called for even rudimentary cognitive skill, a larger reward led to poorer performance. Then they said, "Let's see if there's any cultural bias here.
dan_pink_the_puzzle_of_motivation.zh: 奬勵越大，表現越差，

dan_pink_the_puzzle_of_motivation.en: Let's go to Madurai, India and test it." Standard of living is lower. In Madurai, a reward that is modest in North American standards, is more meaningful there.
dan_pink_the_puzzle_of_motivation.zh: 一個在美國一般的奬勵，

dan_pink_the_puzzle_of_motivation.en: Same deal. A bunch of games, three levels of rewards. What happens? People offered the medium level of rewards did no better than people offered the small rewards.
dan_pink_the_puzzle_of_motivation.zh: 同樣的交易。一堆遊戲，三個等級的獎勵。會發生什麼事？提供中等獎勵的人並不比提供小獎勵的人做得更好。

dan_pink_the_puzzle_of_motivation.en: But this time, people offered the highest rewards, they did the worst of all. In eight of the nine tasks we examined across three experiments, higher incentives led to worse performance.
dan_pink_the_puzzle_of_motivation.zh: 但這一次，人們提供了最高的獎勵，他們卻做得最差。在我們透過三個實驗檢查的九項任務中的八項中，更高的激勵導致了更差的表現。

dan_pink_the_puzzle_of_motivation.en: Is this some kind of touchy-feely socialist conspiracy going on here? No, these are economists from MIT, from Carnegie Mellon, from the University of Chicago. Do you know who sponsored this research?
dan_pink_the_puzzle_of_motivation.zh: 還有，你猜是誰贊助這個研究？

dan_pink_the_puzzle_of_motivation.en: The Federal Reserve Bank of the United States. That's the American experience. Let's go across the pond to the London School of Economics, LSE, London School of Economics,
dan_pink_the_puzzle_of_motivation.zh: 美國聯邦儲備銀行。這就是美國的經驗。讓我們隔著大洋去倫敦政經學院，LSE，倫敦政經學院，

dan_pink_the_puzzle_of_motivation.en: alma mater of eleven Nobel Laureates in economics. Training ground for great economic thinkers like George Soros, and Friedrich Hayek, and Mick Jagger. (Laughter) Last month, just last month,
dan_pink_the_puzzle_of_motivation.zh: 十一位諾貝爾經濟學得主的母校，  例如索羅斯、海耶克、 和米積加，（笑聲）

dan_pink_the_puzzle_of_motivation.en: economists at LSE looked at 51 studies of pay-for-performance plans, inside of companies. Here's what they said: "We find that financial incentives
dan_pink_the_puzzle_of_motivation.zh: 都是圍繞企業内據工作表現決定薪酬的計劃， 經濟學家們的結論是這樣的：

dan_pink_the_puzzle_of_motivation.en: can result in a negative impact on overall performance." There is a mismatch between what science knows and what business does. And what worries me, as we stand here in the rubble
dan_pink_the_puzzle_of_motivation.zh: 使我憂慮的是，我們今天

dan_pink_the_puzzle_of_motivation.en: of the economic collapse, is that too many organizations are making their decisions, their policies about talent and people, based on assumptions that are outdated, unexamined,
dan_pink_the_puzzle_of_motivation.zh: 卻仍有太多的機構， 只憑傳統而非科學驗證的想法，

dan_pink_the_puzzle_of_motivation.en: and rooted more in folklore than in science. And if we really want to get out of this economic mess, if we really want high performance on those definitional tasks of the 21st century,
dan_pink_the_puzzle_of_motivation.zh: 如果我們真正想在二十一世紀的工作上，

dan_pink_the_puzzle_of_motivation.en: the solution is not to do more of the wrong things, to entice people with a sweeter carrot, or threaten them with a sharper stick. We need a whole new approach. The good news is that the scientists
dan_pink_the_puzzle_of_motivation.zh: 好消息是，

dan_pink_the_puzzle_of_motivation.en: who've been studying motivation have given us this new approach. It's built much more around intrinsic motivation. Around the desire to do things because they matter,
dan_pink_the_puzzle_of_motivation.zh: 那些研究行為動機的科學家，已經給了我們答案，

dan_pink_the_puzzle_of_motivation.en: because we like it, they're interesting, or part of something important. And to my mind, that new operating system for our businesses revolves around three elements: autonomy, mastery and purpose.
dan_pink_the_puzzle_of_motivation.zh: 圍繞三個元素，

dan_pink_the_puzzle_of_motivation.en: Autonomy: the urge to direct our own lives. Mastery: the desire to get better and better at something that matters. Purpose: the yearning to do what we do
dan_pink_the_puzzle_of_motivation.zh: 自主性是主宰自己生命的動力， 掌握度，是對自己重視的工作有做好的願望，

dan_pink_the_puzzle_of_motivation.en: in the service of something larger than ourselves. These are the building blocks of an entirely new operating system for our businesses. I want to talk today only about autonomy.
dan_pink_the_puzzle_of_motivation.zh: 能對個人以外有所貢獻的渴望，   我今天只想談一談自主性，

dan_pink_the_puzzle_of_motivation.en: In the 20th century, we came up with this idea of management. Management did not emanate from nature. Management is not a tree, it's a television set. Somebody invented it.
dan_pink_the_puzzle_of_motivation.zh: 二十世紀，冒出了「管理」這個概念， 管理並非由自然產生出來，  它是由人發明出來的，

dan_pink_the_puzzle_of_motivation.en: It doesn't mean it's going to work forever. Management is great. Traditional notions of management are great if you want compliance. But if you want engagement, self-direction works better.
dan_pink_the_puzzle_of_motivation.zh: 但如果你要對工作投入，自我主導會更見效，

dan_pink_the_puzzle_of_motivation.en: Some examples of some kind of radical notions of self-direction. You don't see a lot of it, but you see the first stirrings of something really interesting going on,
dan_pink_the_puzzle_of_motivation.zh: 一些自我指導的激進概念的例子。你看不到很多，但你會看到一些真正有趣的事情正在發生，

dan_pink_the_puzzle_of_motivation.en: what it means is paying people adequately and fairly, absolutely -- getting the issue of money off the table, and then giving people lots of autonomy. Some examples.
dan_pink_the_puzzle_of_motivation.zh: 這意味著給人們足夠的、公平的、絕對的報酬──把金錢問題排除在外，然後給予人們很大的自主權。一些例子。

dan_pink_the_puzzle_of_motivation.en: How many of you have heard of the company Atlassian? It looks like less than half. (Laughter) Atlassian is an Australian software company. And they do something incredibly cool.
dan_pink_the_puzzle_of_motivation.zh: 你們有沒有聽過一間叫阿拉斯安的公司？  （笑聲）  他們做的東西真夠酷，

dan_pink_the_puzzle_of_motivation.en: A few times a year they tell their engineers, "Go for the next 24 hours and work on anything you want, as long as it's not part of your regular job. Work on anything you want."
dan_pink_the_puzzle_of_motivation.zh: 他們每年都會告訴工程師幾次，“在接下來的 24 小時裡，做任何你想做的事情，只要它不是你常規工作的一部分。做任何你想做的事情。”

dan_pink_the_puzzle_of_motivation.en: Engineers use this time to come up with a cool patch for code, come up with an elegant hack. Then they present all of the stuff that they've developed to their teammates, to the rest of the company,
dan_pink_the_puzzle_of_motivation.zh: 工程師利用這段時間來開發一個很酷的程式碼補丁，想出一個優雅的技巧。然後他們向團隊成員、公司其他成員展示他們開發的所有東西，

dan_pink_the_puzzle_of_motivation.en: in this wild and woolly all-hands meeting at the end of the day. Being Australians, everybody has a beer. They call them FedEx Days. Why? Because you have to deliver something overnight.
dan_pink_the_puzzle_of_motivation.zh: 在一天結束時舉行的這場瘋狂而混亂的全體會議上。作為澳洲人，每個人都喝啤酒。他們稱之為聯邦快遞日。為什麼？因為你必須連夜交付一些東西。

dan_pink_the_puzzle_of_motivation.en: It's pretty; not bad. It's a huge trademark violation, but it's pretty clever. (Laughter) That one day of intense autonomy has produced a whole array of software fixes that might never have existed.
dan_pink_the_puzzle_of_motivation.zh: 很漂亮；不錯。這是一個巨大的商標侵權行為，但它非常聰明。 （笑聲）有一天，高度自治已經產生了一系列可能從未存在過的軟體修復。

dan_pink_the_puzzle_of_motivation.en: It's worked so well that Atlassian has taken it to the next level with 20% time -- done, famously, at Google -- where engineers can spend 20% of their time working on anything they want.
dan_pink_the_puzzle_of_motivation.zh: 發現這個方法的好處，阿拉斯安把它提升到更高的層次，

dan_pink_the_puzzle_of_motivation.en: They have autonomy over their time, their task, their team, their technique. Radical amounts of autonomy. And at Google, as many of you know, about half of the new products in a typical year
dan_pink_the_puzzle_of_motivation.zh: 他們對自己的時間、任務、團隊和技術擁有自主權。極大的自主權。正如你們許多人所知，在谷歌，一年中大約有一半的新產品

dan_pink_the_puzzle_of_motivation.en: are birthed during that 20% time: things like Gmail, Orkut, Google News. Let me give you an even more radical example of it: something called the Results Only Work Environment (the ROWE),
dan_pink_the_puzzle_of_motivation.zh: 讓我再舉一個更革命性的例子，

dan_pink_the_puzzle_of_motivation.en: created by two American consultants, in place at a dozen companies around North America. In a ROWE people don't have schedules. They show up when they want.
dan_pink_the_puzzle_of_motivation.zh: 由兩名美國顧問創建，在北美各地的十幾家公司都有分佈。在 ROWE 中，人們沒有日程安排。他們會在需要的時候出現。

dan_pink_the_puzzle_of_motivation.en: They don't have to be in the office at a certain time, or any time. They just have to get their work done. How they do it, when they do it, where they do it, is totally up to them.
dan_pink_the_puzzle_of_motivation.zh: 他們不必在特定時間或任何時間出現在辦公室。他們只需要完成他們的工作。他們如何做、何時做、在哪裡做，完全取決於他們。

dan_pink_the_puzzle_of_motivation.en: Meetings in these kinds of environments are optional. What happens? Almost across the board, productivity goes up, worker engagement goes up, worker satisfaction goes up, turnover goes down.
dan_pink_the_puzzle_of_motivation.zh: 在此類環境中召開會議是可選的。會發生什麼事？幾乎所有方面，生產力都提高了，員工敬業度提高了，員工滿意度提高了，人員流動率下降了。

dan_pink_the_puzzle_of_motivation.en: Autonomy, mastery and purpose, the building blocks of a new way of doing things. Some of you might look at this and say, "Hmm, that sounds nice, but it's Utopian." And I say, "Nope. I have proof."
dan_pink_the_puzzle_of_motivation.zh: 自主性、掌握度、使命感， 這都是新工作方式的基本單元，  「嗯，雖然很好聽，但那是烏托邦，並不存在的。」

dan_pink_the_puzzle_of_motivation.en: The mid-1990s, Microsoft started an encyclopedia called Encarta. They had deployed all the right incentives, They paid professionals to write and edit thousands of articles.
dan_pink_the_puzzle_of_motivation.zh: 他們調動了最理想的獎勵，

dan_pink_the_puzzle_of_motivation.en: Well-compensated managers oversaw the whole thing to make sure it came in on budget and on time. A few years later, another encyclopedia got started. Different model, right? Do it for fun.
dan_pink_the_puzzle_of_motivation.zh: 幾年後，另外一個百科全書誕生了，

dan_pink_the_puzzle_of_motivation.en: No one gets paid a cent, or a euro or a yen. Do it because you like to do it. Just 10 years ago, if you had gone to an economist, anywhere,
dan_pink_the_puzzle_of_motivation.zh: 如果你問任何一個經濟學家，

dan_pink_the_puzzle_of_motivation.en: "Hey, I've got these two different models for creating an encyclopedia. If they went head to head, who would win?" 10 years ago you could not have found a single sober economist
dan_pink_the_puzzle_of_motivation.zh: 「嘿，我有這兩種不同的模型來創建百科全書。如果他們正面交鋒，誰會贏？」十年前你不可能找到一個清醒的經濟學家

dan_pink_the_puzzle_of_motivation.en: anywhere on planet Earth who would have predicted the Wikipedia model. This is the titanic battle between these two approaches. This is the Ali-Frazier of motivation, right?
dan_pink_the_puzzle_of_motivation.zh: 地球上任何能夠預測維基百科模型的人。這是這兩種方法之間的激烈戰鬥。這就是阿里-弗雷澤的動機，對嗎？

dan_pink_the_puzzle_of_motivation.en: This is the Thrilla in Manila. Intrinsic motivators versus extrinsic motivators. Autonomy, mastery and purpose, versus carrot and sticks, and who wins?
dan_pink_the_puzzle_of_motivation.zh: 對抗獎與罰，誰勝?

dan_pink_the_puzzle_of_motivation.en: Intrinsic motivation, autonomy, mastery and purpose, in a knockout. Let me wrap up. There is a mismatch between what science knows and what business does. Here is what science knows.
dan_pink_the_puzzle_of_motivation.zh: 淘汰賽中的內在動機、自主性、掌控力和目標。讓我總結一下。 There is a mismatch between what science knows and what business does.這是科學所知道的。

dan_pink_the_puzzle_of_motivation.en: One: Those 20th century rewards, those motivators we think are a natural part of business, do work, but only in a surprisingly narrow band of circumstances.
dan_pink_the_puzzle_of_motivation.zh: 一：那些 20 世紀的獎勵，那些我們認為是商業自然組成部分的激勵因素，確實有效，但只在非常有限的情況下有效。

dan_pink_the_puzzle_of_motivation.en: Two: Those if-then rewards often destroy creativity. Three: The secret to high performance isn't rewards and punishments, but that unseen intrinsic drive-- the drive to do things for their own sake.
dan_pink_the_puzzle_of_motivation.zh: 第二：那些「如果-那麼」獎勵往往會破壞創造力。三：高績效的秘訣不是獎勵和懲罰，而是看不見的內在動力──為了自己的利益而做事的動力。

dan_pink_the_puzzle_of_motivation.en: The drive to do things cause they matter. And here's the best part. We already know this. The science confirms what we know in our hearts. So, if we repair this mismatch between science and business,
dan_pink_the_puzzle_of_motivation.zh: 那種因為工作有意義而做的動力， 最精采的是， 科學已經把我們心知的道理證實，

dan_pink_the_puzzle_of_motivation.en: if we bring our motivation, notions of motivation into the 21st century, if we get past this lazy, dangerous, ideology of carrots and sticks, we can strengthen our businesses,
dan_pink_the_puzzle_of_motivation.zh: 如果我們把我們的動機、動機的概念帶入21世紀，如果我們克服這種懶惰、危險、胡蘿蔔加大棒的意識形態，我們就可以加強我們的業務，

dan_pink_the_puzzle_of_motivation.en: we can solve a lot of those candle problems, and maybe, maybe -- we can change the world. I rest my case. (Applause)
dan_pink_the_puzzle_of_motivation.zh: 我們能夠改變世界，

shawn_achor_the_happy_secret_to_better_w.en: When I was seven years old and my sister was just five years old, we were playing on top of a bunk bed. I was two years older than my sister at the time -- I mean, I'm two years older than her now --
shawn_achor_the_happy_secret_to_better_w.zh: 在我七歲，我妹妹才五歲時  那時候我比我妹妹大兩歲 --

shawn_achor_the_happy_secret_to_better_w.en: but at the time it meant she had to do everything that I wanted to do, and I wanted to play war. So we were up on top of our bunk beds. And on one side of the bunk bed,
shawn_achor_the_happy_secret_to_better_w.zh: 所以我們爬到上舖

shawn_achor_the_happy_secret_to_better_w.en: I had put out all of my G.I. Joe soldiers and weaponry. And on the other side were all my sister's My Little Ponies ready for a cavalry charge.
shawn_achor_the_happy_secret_to_better_w.zh: 我已經拿出我所有的大兵了。喬士兵和武器裝備。另一邊，我姊姊的所有小馬駒都做好了騎兵衝鋒的準備。

shawn_achor_the_happy_secret_to_better_w.en: There are differing accounts of what actually happened that afternoon, but since my sister is not here with us today, let me tell you the true story -- (Laughter)
shawn_achor_the_happy_secret_to_better_w.zh: 對於那天下午實際發生的事情有不同的說法，但由於我姐姐今天不和我們在一起，所以讓我告訴你真實的故事——（笑聲）

shawn_achor_the_happy_secret_to_better_w.en: which is my sister's a little on the clumsy side. Somehow, without any help or push from her older brother at all, Amy disappeared off of the top of the bunk bed
shawn_achor_the_happy_secret_to_better_w.zh: 當時我妹妹正節節敗退

shawn_achor_the_happy_secret_to_better_w.en: and landed with this crash on the floor. I nervously peered over the side of the bed to see what had befallen my fallen sister and saw that she had landed painfully on her hands and knees
shawn_achor_the_happy_secret_to_better_w.zh: 我看到我妹妹趴在地上，五體投地的樣子

shawn_achor_the_happy_secret_to_better_w.en: on all fours on the ground. I was nervous because my parents had charged me with making sure that my sister and I played as safely and as quietly as possible.
shawn_achor_the_happy_secret_to_better_w.zh: 趴在地上。我很緊張，因為我的父母責成我確保我和妹妹盡可能安全、安靜地玩耍。

shawn_achor_the_happy_secret_to_better_w.en: And seeing as how I had accidentally broken Amy's arm just one week before -- (Laughter) (Laughter ends) heroically pushing her out of the way of an oncoming imaginary sniper bullet, (Laughter)
shawn_achor_the_happy_secret_to_better_w.zh: 因為我上星期才不小心  （笑聲）

shawn_achor_the_happy_secret_to_better_w.en: for which I have yet to be thanked, I was trying as hard as I could -- she didn't even see it coming -- I was trying hard to be on my best behavior. And I saw my sister's face,
shawn_achor_the_happy_secret_to_better_w.zh: 為此我還沒有得到感謝，我已經盡了最大的努力——她甚至沒有預見到這一點——我正在努力表現出最好的行為。我看到了姐姐的臉，

shawn_achor_the_happy_secret_to_better_w.en: this wail of pain and suffering and surprise threatening to erupt from her mouth and wake my parents from the long winter's nap for which they had settled. So I did the only thing
shawn_achor_the_happy_secret_to_better_w.zh: 這聲痛苦、折磨和驚訝的哭聲威脅著要從她嘴裡爆發出來，把我父母從他們已經習慣的漫長冬日小睡中吵醒。所以我做了唯一的一件事

shawn_achor_the_happy_secret_to_better_w.en: my frantic seven year-old brain could think to do to avert this tragedy. And if you have children, you've seen this hundreds of times. I said, "Amy, wait. Don't cry. Did you see how you landed?
shawn_achor_the_happy_secret_to_better_w.zh: 我七歲的大腦瘋狂地想如何避免這場悲劇。如果你有孩子，你已經見過數百次這種情況了。我說：「艾米，等等。別哭。你看到你是怎麼著陸的嗎？

shawn_achor_the_happy_secret_to_better_w.en: No human lands on all fours like that. Amy, I think this means you're a unicorn." (Laughter) Now, that was cheating, because there was nothing she would want more
shawn_achor_the_happy_secret_to_better_w.zh: （笑聲）

shawn_achor_the_happy_secret_to_better_w.en: than not to be Amy the hurt five year-old little sister, but Amy the special unicorn. Of course, this option was open to her brain at no point in the past.
shawn_achor_the_happy_secret_to_better_w.zh: 不是艾米那個受傷的五歲小妹妹，而是艾米那隻特殊的獨角獸。當然，她的大腦在過去從未有過這種選擇。

shawn_achor_the_happy_secret_to_better_w.en: And you could see how my poor, manipulated sister faced conflict, as her little brain attempted to devote resources to feeling the pain and suffering and surprise she just experienced,
shawn_achor_the_happy_secret_to_better_w.zh: 你可以看到我可憐的、被操縱的妹妹如何面對衝突，因為她的小大腦試圖投入資源來感受她剛剛經歷的痛苦和驚訝，

shawn_achor_the_happy_secret_to_better_w.en: or contemplating her new-found identity as a unicorn. And the latter won. Instead of crying or ceasing our play, instead of waking my parents, with all the negative consequences for me,
shawn_achor_the_happy_secret_to_better_w.zh: 所有可能發生在我身上的 負面後果都沒有發生

shawn_achor_the_happy_secret_to_better_w.en: a smile spread across her face and she scrambled back up onto the bunk bed with all the grace of a baby unicorn -- (Laughter) with one broken leg. What we stumbled across
shawn_achor_the_happy_secret_to_better_w.zh: 然後以小獨角獸的 翩翩姿態爬回雙層床去 ...

shawn_achor_the_happy_secret_to_better_w.en: at this tender age of just five and seven -- we had no idea at the time -- was was going be at the vanguard of a scientific revolution
shawn_achor_the_happy_secret_to_better_w.zh: 在這個只有五歲和七歲的年紀──我們當時並不知道──將成為科學革命的先鋒

shawn_achor_the_happy_secret_to_better_w.en: occurring two decades later in the way that we look at the human brain. We had stumbled across something called positive psychology, which is the reason I'm here today
shawn_achor_the_happy_secret_to_better_w.zh: 二十年後以我們看待人類大腦的方式發生了。我們偶然發現了一種叫做正向心理學的東西，這就是我今天來這裡的原因

shawn_achor_the_happy_secret_to_better_w.en: and the reason that I wake up every morning. When I started talking about this research outside of academia, with companies and schools, the first thing they said to never do is to start with a graph.
shawn_achor_the_happy_secret_to_better_w.zh: 以及我每天早上醒來的原因。當我開始在學術界之外、公司和學校談論這項研究時，他們說永遠不要做的第一件事就是從圖表開始。

shawn_achor_the_happy_secret_to_better_w.en: The first thing I want to do is start with a graph. This graph looks boring, but it is the reason I get excited and wake up every morning. And this graph doesn't even mean anything; it's fake data.
shawn_achor_the_happy_secret_to_better_w.zh: 但這張圖正是我每天 早上能興奮的起床的原因

shawn_achor_the_happy_secret_to_better_w.en: What we found is -- (Laughter) If I got this data studying you, I would be thrilled, because there's a trend there, and that means that I can get published, which is all that really matters.
shawn_achor_the_happy_secret_to_better_w.zh: 如果這張圖是我研究 在場各位的結果，我會很激動 因為這張圖的趨勢非常明顯 代表著我可以發表這個研究結果 而發表結果是我做研究最主要的原因

shawn_achor_the_happy_secret_to_better_w.en: There is one weird red dot above the curve, there's one weirdo in the room -- I know who you are, I saw you earlier -- that's no problem.
shawn_achor_the_happy_secret_to_better_w.zh: 但有一個怪怪的小紅點在曲線的上方

shawn_achor_the_happy_secret_to_better_w.en: That's no problem, as most of you know, because I can just delete that dot. I can delete that dot because that's clearly a measurement error.
shawn_achor_the_happy_secret_to_better_w.zh: 真的沒關係，大部份的人都知道這不影響 我可以刪掉它，很明顯它是個測量偏誤

shawn_achor_the_happy_secret_to_better_w.en: And we know that's a measurement error because it's messing up my data. (Laughter) So one of the first things we teach people in economics, statistics, business and psychology courses
shawn_achor_the_happy_secret_to_better_w.zh: 我們知道這是一個測量錯誤，因為它弄亂了我的數據。 （笑聲）所以我們在經濟學、統計學、商業和心理學課程中教給人們的第一件事就是

shawn_achor_the_happy_secret_to_better_w.en: is how, in a statistically valid way, do we eliminate the weirdos. How do we eliminate the outliers so we can find the line of best fit? Which is fantastic if I'm trying to find out
shawn_achor_the_happy_secret_to_better_w.zh: 就是我們如何以統計上有效的方式消除怪人。我們如何消除異常值以便找到最佳擬合線？如果我想找出答案那就太棒了

shawn_achor_the_happy_secret_to_better_w.en: how many Advil the average person should be taking -- two. But if I'm interested in your potential, or for happiness or productivity or energy or creativity,
shawn_achor_the_happy_secret_to_better_w.zh: 一般人應該服用多少 Advil——兩片。但如果我對你的潛力感興趣，或對幸福、生產力、精力或創造力感興趣，

shawn_achor_the_happy_secret_to_better_w.en: we're creating the cult of the average with science. If I asked a question like, "How fast can a child learn how to read in a classroom?" scientists change the answer to
shawn_achor_the_happy_secret_to_better_w.zh: 這種科學方法創造出 我們對平均數的一種迷思  「在課堂裡，一個學童多快可以學會閱讀？」

shawn_achor_the_happy_secret_to_better_w.en: "How fast does the average child learn how to read in that classroom?" and we tailor the class towards the average. If you fall below the average, then psychologists get thrilled,
shawn_achor_the_happy_secret_to_better_w.zh: 「普通孩子在教室裡學習閱讀的速度有多快？」我們會根據平均值調整班級。如果你的成績低於平均水平，心理學家會很興奮，

shawn_achor_the_happy_secret_to_better_w.en: because that means you're depressed or have a disorder, or hopefully both. We're hoping for both because our business model is, if you come into a therapy session with one problem,
shawn_achor_the_happy_secret_to_better_w.zh: 我們希望看到兩者都有 因為我們的商業模式是

shawn_achor_the_happy_secret_to_better_w.en: we want to make sure you leave knowing you have ten, so you keep coming back. We'll go back into your childhood if necessary, but eventually we want to make you normal again.
shawn_achor_the_happy_secret_to_better_w.zh: 我們希望確保您離開時知道自己有十個，這樣您就會不斷回來。如有必要，我們會回到你的童年，但最終我們想讓你恢復正常。

shawn_achor_the_happy_secret_to_better_w.en: But normal is merely average. And positive psychology posits that if we study what is merely average, we will remain merely average. Then instead of deleting those positive outliers,
shawn_achor_the_happy_secret_to_better_w.zh: 但正常只是平均。正向心理學認為，如果我們只是研究平均水平，我們就只能保持平均水平。然後，不要刪除那些正異常值，

shawn_achor_the_happy_secret_to_better_w.en: what I intentionally do is come into a population like this one and say, why? Why are some of you high above the curve in terms of intellectual, athletic, musical ability, creativity, energy levels,
shawn_achor_the_happy_secret_to_better_w.zh: 我故意做的就是走進這樣的人群並問，為什麼？為什麼你們中的一些人在智力、運動、音樂能力、創造力、精力水平方面都高於曲線，

shawn_achor_the_happy_secret_to_better_w.en: resiliency in the face of challenge, sense of humor? Whatever it is, instead of deleting you, what I want to do is study you. Because maybe we can glean information,
shawn_achor_the_happy_secret_to_better_w.zh: 面對挑戰的韌性、幽默感？不管怎樣，我不想刪除你，而是想研究你。因為也許我們可以收集信息，

shawn_achor_the_happy_secret_to_better_w.en: not just how to move people up to the average, but move the entire average up in our companies and schools worldwide. The reason this graph is important to me
shawn_achor_the_happy_secret_to_better_w.zh: not just how to move people up to the average, but move the entire average up in our companies and schools worldwide.這張圖對我來說很重要的原因

shawn_achor_the_happy_secret_to_better_w.en: is, on the news, the majority of the information is not positive. in fact it's negative. Most of it's about murder, corruption, diseases, natural disasters. And very quickly, my brain starts to think
shawn_achor_the_happy_secret_to_better_w.zh: 就是，新聞上，大部分的資訊都不是正面。事實上它是負面的。其中大部分是關於謀殺、腐敗、疾病、天災。很快，我的大腦開始思考

shawn_achor_the_happy_secret_to_better_w.en: that's the accurate ratio of negative to positive in the world. This creates "the medical school syndrome." During the first year of medical training,
shawn_achor_the_happy_secret_to_better_w.zh: 這就是世界上消極與積極的準確比率。這就造成了「醫學院症候群」。在醫學訓練的第一年，

shawn_achor_the_happy_secret_to_better_w.en: as you read through a list of all the symptoms and diseases, suddenly you realize you have all of them. (Laughter) I have a brother in-law named Bobo, which is a whole other story.
shawn_achor_the_happy_secret_to_better_w.zh: 當您閱讀所有症狀和疾病的清單時，您突然意識到自己患有所有這些症狀和疾病。 （笑聲）我有一個姐夫，名叫波波，這是一個完全不同的故事。

shawn_achor_the_happy_secret_to_better_w.en: Bobo married Amy the unicorn. Bobo called me on the phone -- (Laughter) from Yale Medical School, and Bobo said, "Shawn, I have leprosy." (Laughter) Which, even at Yale, is extraordinarily rare.
shawn_achor_the_happy_secret_to_better_w.zh: 布波說：「紹恩，我得了痲瘋病。」  這個病在耶魯也很罕見

shawn_achor_the_happy_secret_to_better_w.en: But I had no idea how to console poor Bobo because he had just gotten over an entire week of menopause. (Laughter) We're finding it's not necessarily the reality that shapes us,
shawn_achor_the_happy_secret_to_better_w.zh: 因為他才從一整個星期的 停經症狀中恢復過來  我們發現，我們並不盡然由事實所塑造

shawn_achor_the_happy_secret_to_better_w.en: but the lens through which your brain views the world that shapes your reality. And if we can change the lens, not only can we change your happiness,
shawn_achor_the_happy_secret_to_better_w.zh: 塑造我們的其實是那副 「你的腦子如何看世界」的鏡片

shawn_achor_the_happy_secret_to_better_w.en: we can change every single educational and business outcome at the same time. I applied to Harvard on a dare. I didn't expect to get in, and my family had no money for college.
shawn_achor_the_happy_secret_to_better_w.zh: 我們可以同時改變每項教育和商業成果。我大膽地申請了哈佛。沒想到能考上，家裡也沒錢上大學。

shawn_achor_the_happy_secret_to_better_w.en: When I got a military scholarship two weeks later, they let me go. Something that wasn't even a possibility became a reality. I assumed everyone there would see it as a privilege as well,
shawn_achor_the_happy_secret_to_better_w.zh: 兩週後，當我獲得軍事獎學金時，他們讓我走了。一些原本不可能的事情變成了現實。我以為那裡的每個人都會認為這是一種特權，

shawn_achor_the_happy_secret_to_better_w.en: that they'd be excited to be there. Even in a classroom full of people smarter than you, I felt you'd be happy just to be in that classroom. But what I found is, while some people experience that,
shawn_achor_the_happy_secret_to_better_w.zh: 他們會很高興能在那裡。即使教室裡擠滿了比你聰明的人，我覺得只要在那間教室裡你就會很高興。但我發現，雖然有些人經歷過這種情況，

shawn_achor_the_happy_secret_to_better_w.en: when I graduated after my four years and then spent the next eight years living in the dorms with the students -- Harvard asked me to; I wasn't that guy. (Laughter)
shawn_achor_the_happy_secret_to_better_w.zh: 當我四年後畢業，然後在接下來的八年裡和學生們一起住在宿舍時——哈佛要求我這樣做；我不是那個人。 （笑聲）

shawn_achor_the_happy_secret_to_better_w.en: I was an officer to counsel students through the difficult four years. And in my research and my teaching, I found that these students, no matter how happy they were
shawn_achor_the_happy_secret_to_better_w.zh: 哈佛聘雇我當指導老師 幫助學生度過這困難的四年

shawn_achor_the_happy_secret_to_better_w.en: with their original success of getting into the school, two weeks later their brains were focused, not on the privilege of being there, nor on their philosophy or physics,
shawn_achor_the_happy_secret_to_better_w.zh: 隨著他們最初成功進入學校，兩週後，他們的大腦不再關注在那裡的特權，也不是他們的哲學或物理學，

shawn_achor_the_happy_secret_to_better_w.en: but on the competition, the workload, the hassles, stresses, complaints. When I first went in there, I walked into the freshmen dining hall,
shawn_achor_the_happy_secret_to_better_w.zh: 但關於競爭、工作量、麻煩、壓力、抱怨。當我第一次進去的時候，我走進了新生食堂，

shawn_achor_the_happy_secret_to_better_w.en: which is where my friends from Waco, Texas, which is where I grew up -- I know some of you know this. When they'd visit, they'd look around,
shawn_achor_the_happy_secret_to_better_w.zh: 這是我來自德克薩斯州韋科的朋友們的所在地，也是我長大的地方——我知道你們中的一些人知道這一點。當他們來訪時，他們會環顧四周，

shawn_achor_the_happy_secret_to_better_w.en: and say, "This dining hall looks like something out of Hogwart's." It does, because that was Hogwart's and that's Harvard. And when they see this,
shawn_achor_the_happy_secret_to_better_w.zh: 並說：「這家餐廳看起來就像是霍格華茲的餐廳。」確實如此，因為那是霍格華茲的，那是哈佛的。當他們看到這一點時，

shawn_achor_the_happy_secret_to_better_w.en: they say, "Why do you waste your time studying happiness at Harvard? What does a Harvard student possibly have to be unhappy about?" Embedded within that question
shawn_achor_the_happy_secret_to_better_w.zh: 不快樂啊？」

shawn_achor_the_happy_secret_to_better_w.en: is the key to understanding the science of happiness. Because what that question assumes is that our external world is predictive of our happiness levels,
shawn_achor_the_happy_secret_to_better_w.zh: 是理解幸福科學的關鍵。因為這個問題假設我們的外在世界可以預測我們的幸福水平，

shawn_achor_the_happy_secret_to_better_w.en: when in reality, if I know everything about your external world, I can only predict 10% of your long-term happiness. 90 percent of your long-term happiness is predicted not by the external world,
shawn_achor_the_happy_secret_to_better_w.zh: 事實上，如果我了解你外在世界的一切，我只能預測你10%的長期幸福。你90%的長期幸福不是外在世界預測的，

shawn_achor_the_happy_secret_to_better_w.en: but by the way your brain processes the world. And if we change it, if we change our formula for happiness and success, we can change the way that we can then affect reality.
shawn_achor_the_happy_secret_to_better_w.zh: 但順便說一句，你的大腦處理世界的方式。如果我們改變它，如果我們改變幸福和成功的公式，我們就可以改變我們影響現實的方式。

shawn_achor_the_happy_secret_to_better_w.en: What we found is that only 25% of job successes are predicted by IQ, 75 percent of job successes are predicted by your optimism levels, your social support
shawn_achor_the_happy_secret_to_better_w.zh: 另外要預測75%的工作成就 就看你的樂觀程度，你的社交圈

shawn_achor_the_happy_secret_to_better_w.en: and your ability to see stress as a challenge instead of as a threat. I talked to a New England boarding school, probably the most prestigious one, and they said, "We already know that.
shawn_achor_the_happy_secret_to_better_w.zh: 還有你能不能把壓力當作挑戰，而不是威脅

shawn_achor_the_happy_secret_to_better_w.en: So every year, instead of just teaching our students, we have a wellness week. And we're so excited. Monday night we have the world's leading expert will speak about adolescent depression.
shawn_achor_the_happy_secret_to_better_w.zh: 因此，每年我們不僅會教導學生，還會舉辦健康週。我們非常興奮。週一晚上，我們將邀請世界領先的專家談論青少年憂鬱症。

shawn_achor_the_happy_secret_to_better_w.en: Tuesday night it's school violence and bullying. Wednesday night is eating disorders. Thursday night is illicit drug use. And Friday night we're trying to decide between risky sex or happiness."
shawn_achor_the_happy_secret_to_better_w.zh: 週二晚上是校園暴力和霸凌事件。週三晚上是飲食失調的日子。週四晚上是非法吸毒的日子。週五晚上，我們要在危險的性行為和幸福之間做出選擇。 」

shawn_achor_the_happy_secret_to_better_w.en: (Laughter) I said, "That's most people's Friday nights." (Laughter) (Applause) Which I'm glad you liked, but they did not like that at all. Silence on the phone.
shawn_achor_the_happy_secret_to_better_w.zh: 我說：「大部份人的星期五晚上都是這樣的。」  （鼓掌） 我很高興你們喜歡這個笑話，但是他們不喜歡

shawn_achor_the_happy_secret_to_better_w.en: And into the silence, I said, "I'd be happy to speak at your school, but that's not a wellness week, that's a sickness week. You've outlined all the negative things that can happen,
shawn_achor_the_happy_secret_to_better_w.zh: 為了打破僵局，我說： 「我很高興能在你的學校演講

shawn_achor_the_happy_secret_to_better_w.en: but not talked about the positive." The absence of disease is not health. Here's how we get to health: We need to reverse the formula for happiness and success.
shawn_achor_the_happy_secret_to_better_w.zh: 但沒有談論正面的一面。 「沒有疾病並不代表健康。我們如何獲得健康：我們需要扭轉幸福和成功的公式。

shawn_achor_the_happy_secret_to_better_w.en: In the last three years, I've traveled to 45 countries, working with schools and companies in the midst of an economic downturn. And I found that most companies and schools
shawn_achor_the_happy_secret_to_better_w.zh: 在過去的三年裡，我去過 45 個國家，在經濟低迷時期與學校和公司合作。我發現大多數公司和學校

shawn_achor_the_happy_secret_to_better_w.en: follow a formula for success, which is this: If I work harder, I'll be more successful. And if I'm more successful, then I'll be happier. That undergirds most of our parenting and managing styles,
shawn_achor_the_happy_secret_to_better_w.zh: 遵循一個成功的公式，那就是：如果我更努力，我會更成功。如果我更成功，我就會更快樂。這鞏固了我們大多數的養育和管理風格，

shawn_achor_the_happy_secret_to_better_w.en: the way that we motivate our behavior. And the problem is it's scientifically broken and backwards for two reasons. Every time your brain has a success,
shawn_achor_the_happy_secret_to_better_w.zh: 我們激勵我們行為的方式。問題是它在科學上是被破壞和倒退的，原因有二。每當你的大腦取得成功時，

shawn_achor_the_happy_secret_to_better_w.en: you just changed the goalpost of what success looked like. You got good grades, now you have to get better grades, you got into a good school and after you get into a better one,
shawn_achor_the_happy_secret_to_better_w.zh: 你只是改變了成功的目標。你取得了好成績，現在你必須取得更好的成績，你進入了一所好學校，在你進入一所更好的學校之後，

shawn_achor_the_happy_secret_to_better_w.en: you got a good job, now you have to get a better job, you hit your sales target, we're going to change it. And if happiness is on the opposite side of success, your brain never gets there.
shawn_achor_the_happy_secret_to_better_w.zh: 你找到了一份好工作，現在你必須找到一份更好的工作，你達到了銷售目標，我們將改變它。如果幸福與成功相反，你的大腦永遠不會到達那裡。

shawn_achor_the_happy_secret_to_better_w.en: We've pushed happiness over the cognitive horizon, as a society. And that's because we think we have to be successful, then we'll be happier. But our brains work in the opposite order.
shawn_achor_the_happy_secret_to_better_w.zh: 作為一個社會，我們已經將幸福超越了認知範圍。那是因為我們認為我們必須成功，然後我們才會更快樂。但我們的大腦以相反的順序運作。

shawn_achor_the_happy_secret_to_better_w.en: If you can raise somebody's level of positivity in the present, then their brain experiences what we now call a happiness advantage, which is your brain at positive performs significantly better
shawn_achor_the_happy_secret_to_better_w.zh: 如果你能提高某人目前的積極性水平，那麼他們的大腦就會體驗到我們現在所說的幸福優勢，即你的大腦在積極性方面表現得明顯更好

shawn_achor_the_happy_secret_to_better_w.en: than at negative, neutral or stressed. Your intelligence rises, your creativity rises, your energy levels rise. In fact, we've found that every single business outcome improves.
shawn_achor_the_happy_secret_to_better_w.zh: 比消極、中立或有壓力的情況好。你的智力提高了，你的創造力提高了，你的能量水平提高了。事實上，我們發現每一項業務成果都得到了改善。

shawn_achor_the_happy_secret_to_better_w.en: Your brain at positive is 31% more productive than your brain at negative, neutral or stressed. You're 37% better at sales. Doctors are 19 percent faster, more accurate
shawn_achor_the_happy_secret_to_better_w.zh: 處於正面狀態的大腦比處於負面、中性或壓力狀態時的大腦生產力高出 31%。您的銷售業績提高了 37%。醫生的速度提高了 19%，準確性也提高了

shawn_achor_the_happy_secret_to_better_w.en: at coming up with the correct diagnosis when positive instead of negative, neutral or stressed. Which means we can reverse the formula. If we can find a way of becoming positive in the present,
shawn_achor_the_happy_secret_to_better_w.zh: 當積極而不是消極、中性或有壓力時得出正確的診斷。這意味著我們可以反轉公式。如果我們能找到讓當下變得積極的方法

shawn_achor_the_happy_secret_to_better_w.en: then our brains work even more successfully as we're able to work harder, faster and more intelligently. We need to be able to reverse this formula
shawn_achor_the_happy_secret_to_better_w.zh: 然後，當我們能夠更努力、更快、更聰明地工作時，我們的大腦就會更成功地運作。我們需要能夠反轉這個公式

shawn_achor_the_happy_secret_to_better_w.en: so we can start to see what our brains are actually capable of. Because dopamine, which floods into your system when you're positive, has two functions. Not only does it make you happier,
shawn_achor_the_happy_secret_to_better_w.zh: 我們才能開始想像我們的腦子 到底能做到什麼地步

shawn_achor_the_happy_secret_to_better_w.en: it turns on all of the learning centers in your brain allowing you to adapt to the world in a different way. We've found there are ways that you can train your brain to be able to become more positive.
shawn_achor_the_happy_secret_to_better_w.zh: 它會打開你腦中所有的學習中心，讓你以不同的方式適應世界。我們發現有一些方法可以訓練你的大腦變得更加積極。

shawn_achor_the_happy_secret_to_better_w.en: In just a two-minute span of time done for 21 days in a row, we can actually rewire your brain, allowing your brain to actually work more optimistically and more successfully.
shawn_achor_the_happy_secret_to_better_w.zh: 在連續 21 天的短短兩分鐘時間內，我們實際上可以重新連接您的大腦，讓您的大腦實際上更樂觀、更成功地工作。

shawn_achor_the_happy_secret_to_better_w.en: We've done these things in research now in every company that I've worked with, getting them to write down three new things that they're grateful for for 21 days in a row, three new things each day.
shawn_achor_the_happy_secret_to_better_w.zh: 我們現在在我合作過的每家公司都做了這些研究，讓他們連續 21 天寫下三件讓他們感激的新事情，每天三件新事情。

shawn_achor_the_happy_secret_to_better_w.en: And at the end of that, their brain starts to retain a pattern of scanning the world not for the negative, but for the positive first.
shawn_achor_the_happy_secret_to_better_w.zh: 最後，他們的大腦開始保留一種掃描世界的模式，首先不是尋找負面的一面，而是尋找正面積極的一面。

shawn_achor_the_happy_secret_to_better_w.en: Journaling about one positive experience you've had over the past 24 hours allows your brain to relive it. Exercise teaches your brain that your behavior matters.
shawn_achor_the_happy_secret_to_better_w.zh: 記錄過去 24 小時內的一次積極經歷可以讓您的大腦重溫它。運動會讓你的大腦知道你的行為很重要。

shawn_achor_the_happy_secret_to_better_w.en: We find that meditation allows your brain to get over the cultural ADHD that we've been creating by trying to do multiple tasks at once and allows our brains to focus on the task at hand.
shawn_achor_the_happy_secret_to_better_w.zh: 我們發現冥想可以讓你的大腦克服我們因試圖同時完成多項任務而造成的文化過動症，並讓我們的大腦專注於手頭上的任務。
